translation: It is easy to understand that the very efficacy of the stars varies in such constitutions of the four seasons, ages, or angles. And indeed, in constitutions congruent with the quality, they acquire a purer and greater efficacy, as in hot things, those that heat, and in moist things, those that moisten. In alien things, however, they are weakened by the admixture of diversity, as in cold things, those that heat, and in dry things, those that moisten, and similarly in others according to the proportion and mixture of qualities. Furthermore, the author seems to be reciting certain opinions rather than establishing doctrine regarding the ages with the quadrants (οικιώod or familiarity). For while some teach that the entire life of a human is contained in the three prior quadrants, so that the first quadrant signifies the first age, the second the middle, the third the end and last part of life, and finally the fourth signifies fame following life: others, however, accommodate the same period of life, divided into four spaces, to the quadrants in the same order: he seems to mix or insinuate both opinions here. But neither of these opinions agrees with the doctrine of Ptolemy, who, although he states that the entire state of life is contained not only in the complete revolution of the world but also in the revolution of individual planets, nevertheless seems to divide the entire life of a human by the ratio of quarters, neither into thirds nor quadrants, but at least into halves, so that the two eastern quadrants, and these compared both to the Sun and to the ascendant, obtain half of life, and the remaining two western quadrants obtain the remaining half of life. For example, in the Moon, two quarters from the new moon and full moon signify half of life, while the remaining quarters opposite these signify the remaining half of life. Similarly, it must be said of the three superior planets. Venus and Mercury, however, although they never come to opposition with the Sun, nevertheless measure the entire life in one period in a small circle, which he calls an epicycle. For their conjunction with the Sun, which occurs while the planet is retrograde, takes the place of the opposition of the three superior planets. But their true synod with the Sun occurs in the upper part of the epicycle where they are swiftest. Ptolemy wrote in this sense in several places, and in the third Apotelesmatic, immediately at the beginning before the treatise on parents, from the position of the significators in the quadrants.

author: Valentin Naibod
title: House Meanings and Triplicity Lords
text: ==Start of OCR for page 1== •PRIMA. 123 fcilicet, ſecundo & de tertio. Vide quis eorú fit fortior effe & loco, hunc facies meritò autorem ſubſtantie & fignificatorem acquifitionis. Quòd ſi fuerit in medio cæ. li, inueniet hanc à rege: & ſi fuerit in domo fidei, aliàs fiducia, eſt melius. Similiter dominus triplicitatis primus dat ſubstantiam in initio vitæ, & ſecundus in medio vitæ, & tertius in fine eius. Tertia domus eſt fratrum & ſororum & propinquo rum ac dilectorum, fidei atque religionis, mandatorum ac legatorum, mutationum atque itinerum minorum: & fignificat eſſe vitæ ante mortem. Dixit Alendez god: Dominus triplicitatis domus fratrum primus, ſignificat fratres maiores, & ſecundus medios, tertius minores, eritque eorum dignitas & eorum eſſe ſecundum loca eorum. Quarta domus eſt patrum, hæreditatum, finis re rum & thefaurorum, & omnium abſconditorum atque occultorum, & ſignat de eſſe vitæ hominum finem. Di xit Alendezgod, quòd dominus triplicitatis domus pa trum primusſignificat patres, ſecundus ciuitates & ter ras, tertius fines rerum & carceres. Quinta domus eſt filiorum & dilectionum, legatorú atque donationum: & ſignificat quid futurum ſit poſt mortem, ex laude ſcilicet & vituperio. Dixit Alendez god, quòd dominus triplicitatis filiorum primus ſignifi cat filios & vitam, ſecundus dilectionem, tertius verò legatos ſeu nuncios. Sexta domus eſt infirmitatum & ſeruorum, & ſig X 3 nificat: ==Start of OCR for page 2== 174 DIFFERENTIA nificat finem vitæ, & quicquid futurum ſit ante ſenectu tem. Dixit Alendez god, dominus triplicitatis domus infirmitatum primus ſignificat infirmitates & valetu dines, ab infirmitatibus & à malis,& deteriorationes: ſecundus fignificat vernaculos & ſeruos: tertius ſigni. ficat, quid eueniet ex eis, & eorum vtilitatem atq; ope. ra:& eſt ſignificator beſtiarum & pecoru, & omnium quadrupedum,& fortitudinis eorú, multitudinis quo. que eorum ac paucitatis, more eorum in manu eius vel egreſſionis eorum ab eaſcilicet manu: carceris quoque atq; retentionis. Septima domus eſt mulierum, nuptiarum, contentio num quoque atque participationum & oppoſitorum. Et fignificat medietatem finis vitæ erga ſenectutem. Di xit Alendezgod, quòd dominus triplicitatis domus ſe. ptima primus ſignificat mulieres, ſecundus contentio nes, tertius commiſtioues & participationes. Octaua domus eſt mortis, ſignificat timorem & mor tem atque Almauerith, id eſt, ſubſtantias vel hæredita tes mortuorum, quas debent hæredes poſt mortem ipſo rum poſsideret:& ſignificat annorum vitæ finem poſt ſe nectutem. Dixit Alendez god,quòd dominus triplicita tis domus mortis primus ſignificat mortem, ſecundus precepta & res antiquas, tertius Almauerith, idest, que hæreditanda ſunt ex mortuis. Nona domus eſt peregrinationum, itenerum, fidei atque religionis, ſapientiæ, philoſophiæ & librorum, epi ſtolarum quoque ac legatorum, narrationum ſeu rumo ram ==Start of OCR for page 3== PRIMA. 175. rum atq; ſomnioru: & initium dimidiæ vitæ ſignificat. Dixit Alendez god,quòd dominus triplicitatis peregri nationis primus ſignificat peregrinationem & omne quod accidit ei:ſecundus ſignificat fidem atque religio. nem & valetudinem harum atque modum earu:tertius eſt ſignificator ſapientiæ, ſomniorum & ſtellarum & au guriorum & veritatis,exercentium ſe in eis, atq; men. dacy. Decima domus eſt regina, & operum, ſublimationis vel exaltationis, regni quoque & memoriæ & vocis, ſci licet imperij,& magiſteriorum atque matrum, & ſigni ficat dimidium annorú vitæ. Dixit Alendez god, quòd dominus triplicitatis domus regiæ primus ſignificat o. pus & exaltationem ſcilicet ſedis, ſublimationem & al tißimam manſionem:ſecundus ſignificat vocem impe ry & audaciam in eodem:tertius ſignificat ſtabilitatem eius atque durabilitatem. Vndecima domus eſt fiduciæ & laudis atque fortu næ, amicorum quoque & miniſtrorum ac auxiliatorum. Et fignificat finem annorum medietatis vitæ. Dixit A. lendez god, quòd dominus triplicitatis domus fiduciæ primus ſignificat fiduciam:ſecundus amicos:tertíus vti litatem ſeu profectus eorum. Duodecima domus eſt inimicorum & laboris atque triſtitia, inuidiæ & ſuſurrationis, qualitatis ingeniorum atque beſtiarum, & ſignificat finem vitæ, & quod con tingit matribus in conceptione ſua ex bono vel malo. Dixit Alendez god, dominus triplicitatis domus ini aucorum ==Start of OCR for page 4== 176 DIFFERENTIA micorum primus ſignificat inimicos, ſecundus labores, tertius verò beſtias & pecora. Hæc ſunt quæ ſignificat XII. domus. Vires domorum ſeu duodecim cæli ſtationum tribus, ijfque diuerfis modis hic conſiderabimus. Primò enim loca cœli per ſe tanto maius habent robur, quanto ampliore & puriore lumine illuſtrantur. Qua de cauſa primum locum obtinet domus decima, ſecundum prima, tertium ſeptima, quartum vndecima, quintum nona, neque de his eſt dubiú. Sextum obtinet duodecima, quia aſcendit, ſeptimum octaua, octauum ſecunda, quia eſt in crepuſculo matutino. Exempli gratia, Coloniævbi polus ſeptentrionalis attollitur part. 51. poſito initio Arietisin aſcendente, cadit in cuſpidem domus ſecundæ pars 24. Tauri, & vltra duas horas oritur part.29. eiufdem Tauri. Id verò cùn ſit tépus crepuſculi matutini, patet quòd pars ſecundæ domus, quæ eſt à 24. parte Tauri ad part.29.eiufdem Tauri, contineatur in matutino crepuſculo. Stellæ ergo in ſecunda domorepertæ vires acquirunt cùm ob ſecundæ domus in luce crepufculina ſitum, tum verò & in primis, quia ſecunda domus eſt in trigona radiatione domus decima, quæ rj dwaudcor cæli exi- ſtens, maximas habet vires.Idcirco Ptolemæus quoque ei magnam vim tribuit, quamuis in ſua regione propter nimis paruá poli eleuationéadmodú exigua eius pars matutino crepufculo contineatur. Nonum locu obtinet ſexta, quia eſt in trigona radiatione decima,& vbiq; gentium veſpertino crepusculo clauditur. Reliquæ domus omnes, quæ funt infra terram & à ſine ſecundæ ad principium ſextæ, continentur lumine, & ob id viribus per ſe carent. Hic verò ſi aliquis quærat, quare quintæ omnis vis adimatur, cùm non minùs quàm ſecunda partem cre puſculi complecti videatur. Huic reſpondendu eſt, domú quin tam cùm decimæ ſit inconiuncta, nulloq; ſchemate conſocietur,&crepusculum illud, vtpote veſpertinum & in tenebras de- ſinens fit longè debilius, & denique pars, quæ contermina eſt ſextæ, non ſit initium quintæ, ſed potius finis, ob debilitatem tantam meritò negligi. Quia tamen dicit Cardanus,eſt in lumine poſita, habet aliquid virium, ſed quæ conſideratione non ſint
translation: Look at which of them is stronger in essence and place; this one you will rightly make the author of substance and the significator of acquisition. If it is in the midheaven, it will find this from the king; and if it is in the house of faith, or rather trust, it is better. Similarly, the first lord of the triplicity gives substance at the beginning of life, the second in the middle of life, and the third at its end. The third house is of brothers and sisters and relatives and loved ones, of faith and religion, of commands and legacies, of changes and minor journeys: and it signifies the essence of life before death. Alendezgod said: The first lord of the triplicity of the house of brothers signifies older brothers, the second middle ones, the third younger ones, and their dignity and their essence will be according to their places. The fourth house is of fathers, inheritances, the end of things and treasures, and all hidden and secret things, and it signifies the end of the essence of human life. Alendezgod said that the first lord of the triplicity of the house of fathers signifies fathers, the second cities and lands, the third the ends of things and prisons. The fifth house is of children and affections, legacies and donations: and it signifies what will happen after death, namely praise and blame. Alendezgod said that the first lord of the triplicity of children signifies children and life, the second affection, the third messengers or envoys. The sixth house is of illnesses and servants, and it signifies the end of life, and whatever will happen before old age. Alendezgod said, the first lord of the triplicity of the house of illnesses signifies illnesses and health, from illnesses and from evils, and deteriorations: the second signifies household slaves and servants: the third signifies what will happen from them, and their usefulness and works: and it is the significator of beasts and cattle, and all quadrupeds, and their strength, also their multitude and scarcity, their manner in his hand or their departure from that hand: also imprisonment and retention. The seventh house is of women, marriages, contentions also and partnerships and opponents. And it signifies the middle of the end of life towards old age. Alendezgod said that the first lord of the triplicity of the seventh house signifies women, the second contentions, the third mixtures and partnerships. The eighth house is of death, it signifies fear and death and Almauerith, that is, the substances or inheritances of the dead, which the heirs ought to possess after their death: and it signifies the end of the years of life after old age. Alendezgod said that the first lord of the triplicity of the house of death signifies death, the second precepts and ancient things, the third Almauerith, that is, what is to be inherited from the dead. The ninth house is of peregrinations, journeys, faith and religion, wisdom, philosophy and books, also letters and legacies, narratives or rumors and dreams: and it signifies the beginning of half of life. Alendezgod said that the first lord of the triplicity of peregrination signifies peregrination and everything that happens to it: the second signifies faith and religion and their health and their manner: the third is the significator of wisdom, dreams and stars and auguries and truth, those who practice them, and falsehood. The tenth house is of the queen, and of works, of exaltation or elevation, also of kingdom and memory and voice, that is, of empire, and of magistracies and mothers, and it signifies half of the years of life. Alendezgod said that the first lord of the triplicity of the royal house signifies work and exaltation, namely of the seat, elevation and the highest dwelling: the second signifies the voice of empire and boldness in the same: the third signifies its stability and durability. The eleventh house is of trust and praise and fortune, also of friends and ministers and helpers. And it signifies the end of the years of the middle of life. Alendezgod said that the first lord of the triplicity of the house of trust signifies trust: the second friends: the third their usefulness or profits. The twelfth house is of enemies and labor and sadness, envy and whispering, the quality of talents and beasts, and it signifies the end of life, and what happens to mothers in their conception, good or bad. Alendezgod said, the first lord of the triplicity of the house of enemies signifies enemies, the second labors, the third beasts and cattle. These are what the twelve houses signify. We will consider the strengths of the houses or the twelve stations of the sky in three different ways here. First, the places of the sky themselves have greater strength the more amply and purely they are illuminated. For this reason, the tenth house holds the first place, the first the second, the seventh the third, the eleventh the fourth, the ninth the fifth, and there is no doubt about these. The twelfth holds the sixth, because it ascends, the eighth the seventh, the second the eighth, because it is in the morning twilight. For example, in Cologne, where the northern pole is elevated 51 degrees, with the beginning of Aries in the ascendant, the cusp of the second house falls at 24 degrees of Taurus, and beyond two hours, 29 degrees of the same Taurus rises. Since this is the time of morning twilight, it is clear that the part of the second house, which is from 24 degrees of Taurus to 29 degrees of the same Taurus, is contained in the morning twilight. Therefore, stars found in the second house acquire strength both because of the position of the second house in the crepuscular light, and indeed primarily because the second house is in a trine radiation with the tenth house, which, being the ruler of the sky, has the greatest strength. Therefore, Ptolemy also attributes great strength to it, although in his region, due to the very small elevation of the pole, a very small part of it is contained in the morning twilight. The sixth house holds the ninth place, because it is in a trine radiation with the tenth, and everywhere it is enclosed in the evening twilight. All other houses, which are below the earth and from the end of the second to the beginning of the sixth, are contained in light, and for this reason lack strength in themselves. Here, if someone asks why all strength is taken away from the fifth house, since it seems to embrace a part of the twilight no less than the second. To this it must be answered that the fifth house, being unconnected to the tenth, and not associated with any scheme, and that twilight, as it is evening and ending in darkness, is far weaker, and finally the part that is contiguous to the sixth is not the beginning of the fifth, but rather the end, due to such weakness it is rightly neglected. However, since Cardanus says it is placed in light, it has some strength, but not to be considered.

author: Valentin Naibod
title: The First House and Nurturing
text: ==Start of OCR for page 3== ` тво DIFFERENTIA quemadmodum Arabes de euentu ab vnaquaque domo iuxta ipforum ſententiam ſignificato ex affectione & diſpoſitione triangulatorum in vniuerſum conijcere & pronunciare ſoleant. Prima domus αναβοφlw, id eſt, nutritionem, vitam & valetudinem corporis tam bonam quam malam ſignificat.Vt igitur exempli gratia, quæ ad educationem nutritionemq; pertinent cognofcant, inquirunt domus primæ, id eſt, ſigni quodin genitura aſcendit dominos triangularitatis, & vbi diurna genitura fuerit, trigonicratora diurnum, & vbi nocturna genitura fuerit, dominum triangularitatis nocturnum primo loco,inde proximè ſequentem, & denique participem conſiderant,atque ex ſitu omnium in themate cœli de bona maláue nutritione conijciunt. Nam ſiomnes reperiantur in angulis & validi, robu ſtam vitam, totamq; nutritionem, quæ primos quatuor annos complectitur, validam declarant. Quòd ſi aliqui quideın ocapent angulos aut ſuccedétes, aliqui verò ab angulis ceciderint: tunc ſinguli triangularitatis domini trientibus nutritionis ſin gulis peculiariter ſignificant ſumpta ratione à triangulatorum ordine. Etenim primo triangulatori nutritionis triens primus, ſecundo ſecúdus, tertio tertius attribuitur. Veluti in exemplo ſuperioris gepiture diurne, in qua afcendit, primarius do minus triplicitatis in angulo occidentis primum trientem nutritionis ſaluum indicat. Hunc ſequitur $,& hunc 4, amboin ſuccedenti loco ſcilicet octauo, in themate cœli domorum 2- qualium,idcirco ſecundum quoque & tertium quadrantem nu tritionis ſaluum, quamuis debiliorem declarant. Quòd ſi omnes fuerint cadentes & impediti, & natiuitas fuerit diurna, ad triangulatores loci Solis: ſi verò nocturna, ad trigonicratoras loci Lunæ, hoc modo vt iam de aſcendente dixi, aſpiciunt.Et ſi nec hic cauſe nutritionis ſufficientes appareant, ad locum con iunctionis vel oppoſitionis immediate natiuitatem præcedentis conſugiunt. Denique ad 4 & & aſpiciunt, quorum vnum Gin angulis vel ſuccedentibus à malis & impedimétis liberum deprehendant, nato nutritionem promittunt. Huius loci eſt enunciatum Hermis 72. λάμβανε τὰ τ' αναβιβῆς ἀλο την βιγα κακρατόρωμ το ωροσκόπο: ΤΑ βίο, ἀπὸ την βιγωνοκρατόρωμτ αίρε Digitized by Google ==End of OCR for page 3== ==Start of OCR for page 4== 1 F PRIMA. 181 αἱρετικό φωτός; id eft, Quæ funt educationis à dominis triangu Laritatis aſcendentis capito: quæ vitæ, à dominis triangularitatis luminaris conditionarij. Enunciati huius duo funt membra. Primum eſt de inueniendo tempore nutritionis,an videlicet illi qui naſcitur, concedatur à calo vt nutriatur vel non nu triatur. Secundum eſt de ijs, quæ cognito tempore nutritionis quæruntur,quçq; ad vniuerſam vita à ſtellis decretam pertinent. Que funt nutritionis, ea à triangulatoribus aſcendentis ſigni, ita vt modò dixi,quærenda funt. Nam cùm aſcendens noſtræ vitæ fit principium, principium quoque educationis non abſurdè ad eiuſdem triangularitatis dominos refer tur. Quæ verò ad reliquam vitam totam, ſi longior nato concedatur, pertinent, vt ſcire poſsis, trigonocratoras loci luminaris conditionarij ſimiliter contemplari oportet. Vocatur au tem luminare aigale feu conditionarium illud, quod temporis conditionem ſequitur. Sicut enim Sol in die, ita Luna in note potentior & efficacior exiſtit. Itaque ſi matiuitas fuerit diurna, triangulatores loci Solis, ſi nocturna, triangulatores loci Lunæ aſpiciédierút:quifi omnes fuerint fortunati, fortes, recepti, in bonis locis & dignitatibus fuis, decernuntnato pro- ſperitaté & diuitias in omnibus diebus vitæ eius. Verbi gratia, in ſequenti exemplo Albohali,genituræ nocturnæ triagulator loci Lunæ ſignú Scorpij luftrantis primus eſt, ſecundus f, tertius, omnes in angulis: Quare huius conſtellationis natus ſemper fælix fuit, ac tandem ad ſublimitatem maximam. cuctus.. Z 3, Quddi Digitized by Google ==End of OCR for page 4== ==Start of OCR for page 5== 182 1 ৫ DIFFEREΝΤΙΑ Genitura no&urna afcendéte Scorpio. جم 69 Quòd ſi vnus fuerit fortunatus, alij verò infortunati, fortunatus quidem ſubſtantiam & diuitias nato promittit ea ætate quz ipſi diſtribuitur: Infortunati verò ca ætate quæ ipfis diuiditur, calamitatem & indigentiam minantur. Primus autem triangulator ſignificat vitę principium, ſeu potius prima tertiam partem vitæ hominis, ſecundus ſecundam:tertius deniqueterrium trientem vitæ hominis adminiſtrat. Veluti in ſequenti ge nitura nocturna, vbi in horoſcopum incidit P.2.fc.30. Librz,& loca planetarum vt vides, ſignificatur ſtatus vitæ à dominis triangularitatis loci Lunæ ſignum Cancri luſtrantis, cuius triangulator primus erat, ſecundus &, ambo cadentes in ſexta & in tertia domo, itaque malum ſtatum vitæ nato minantur. Sed Luna,quæ erat luminare temporis in medio cœli poſita, & ad hoc domina triplicitatis poftrema, & de natura Veneris, pulchritudinem & bonam fortunam nato in fine vitæ decernunt.Nam cùm triangulatores luminaris conditionarij ab an gulis Digitized by Google ==End of OCR for page 5==
translation: The DIFFERENCE, as the Arabs are accustomed to infer and pronounce concerning the outcome from each house, according to their opinion, signified by the affection and disposition of the triplicity rulers in general. The first house signifies 'anabibes', that is, nourishment, life, and the health of the body, both good and bad. Therefore, to understand, for example, what pertains to education and nourishment, they inquire into the lords of the triplicity of the first house, that is, of the sign that ascended in the nativity. If it was a diurnal nativity, they consider the diurnal triplicity ruler; if a nocturnal nativity, the nocturnal triplicity ruler in the first place, then the next one, and finally the participant. From the position of all of them in the celestial chart, they infer about good or bad nourishment. For if all are found in angular houses and are strong, they declare a robust life and strong nourishment for the first four years. But if some occupy angular or succedent houses, and others have fallen from angular houses, then each triplicity ruler signifies particular thirds of nourishment, taken in proportion to the order of the triplicity rulers. For to the first triplicity ruler is attributed the first third of nourishment, to the second the second, and to the third the third. As in the example of the superior diurnal nativity, in which the primary lord of the triplicity in the western angle indicates the first third of nourishment to be safe. This is followed by Jupiter, and this by Saturn, both in the succedent place, namely the eighth, in the celestial chart of equal houses. Therefore, they also declare the second and third quarters of nourishment to be safe, although weaker. But if all were cadent and afflicted, and the nativity was diurnal, they look to the triplicity rulers of the Sun's place; if nocturnal, to the triplicity rulers of the Moon's place, in the way I have already said concerning the ascendant. And if even here sufficient causes for nourishment do not appear, they resort to the place of the conjunction or opposition immediately preceding the nativity. Finally, they look to Jupiter and Venus, one of which, if found in angular or succedent houses, free from evils and impediments, promises nourishment to the native. To this place belongs the statement of Hermes 72: 'Take what pertains to education from the triplicity rulers of the ascendant; what pertains to life, from the triplicity rulers of the conditioned luminary.'

translation: The dictum has two parts. The first is about finding the time of nourishment, whether it is granted by heaven to the native to be nourished or not nourished. The second is about those things that are sought after the time of nourishment is known, and which pertain to the entire life decreed by the stars. What pertains to nourishment must be sought from the triplicity rulers of the ascendant sign, as I have just said. For since the ascendant is the beginning of our life, the beginning of education is not absurdly referred to the lords of its triplicity. But what pertains to the rest of the entire life, if a longer life is granted to the native, so that you may know, you must similarly contemplate the triplicity rulers of the place of the conditioned luminary. The luminary is called 'aigale' or 'conditioned' that which follows the condition of the time. For just as the Sun is more potent and effective by day, so is the Moon by night. Therefore, if the nativity was diurnal, the triplicity rulers of the Sun's place must be looked at; if nocturnal, the triplicity rulers of the Moon's place. If all of these are fortunate, strong, received, in good places and their dignities, they decree prosperity and riches for the native throughout all the days of his life. For example, in the following example of Albohal's nocturnal nativity, the first triplicity ruler of the Moon's place, traversing the sign of Scorpio, is Mars, the second is Jupiter, the third is Saturn, all in angular houses. Therefore, the native of this constellation was always fortunate and eventually rose to the greatest eminence.

author: Valentin Naibod
title: Nocturnal Nativity and Life Status

Prima domus αναβοφlw, id eſt, nutritionem, vitam & valetudinem corporis tam bonam quam malam ſignificat.Vt igitur exempli gratia, quæ ad educationem nutritionemq; pertinent cognofcant, inquirunt domus primæ, id eſt, ſigni quodin genitura aſcendit dominos triangularitatis, & vbi diurna genitura fuerit, trigonicratora diurnum, & vbi nocturna genitura fuerit, dominum triangularitatis nocturnum primo loco,inde proximè ſequentem, & denique participem conſiderant,atque ex ſitu omnium in themate cœli de bona maláue nutritione conijciunt. Nam ſiomnes reperiantur in angulis & validi, robu ſtam vitam, totamq; nutritionem, quæ primos quatuor annos complectitur, validam declarant. Quòd ſi aliqui quideın ocapent angulos aut ſuccedétes, aliqui verò ab angulis ceciderint: tunc ſinguli triangularitatis domini trientibus nutritionis ſin gulis peculiariter ſignificant ſumpta ratione à triangulatorum ordine. Etenim primo triangulatori nutritionis triens primus, ſecundo ſecúdus, tertio tertius attribuitur. Veluti in exemplo ſuperioris gepiture diurne, in qua afcendit, primarius do minus triplicitatis in angulo occidentis primum trientem nutritionis ſaluum indicat. Hunc ſequitur $,& hunc 4, amboin ſuccedenti loco ſcilicet octauo, in themate cœli domorum 2- qualium,idcirco ſecundum quoque & tertium quadrantem nu tritionis ſaluum, quamuis debiliorem declarant. Quòd ſi omnes fuerint cadentes & impediti, & natiuitas fuerit diurna, ad triangulatores loci Solis: ſi verò nocturna, ad trigonicratoras loci Lunæ, hoc modo vt iam de aſcendente dixi, aſpiciunt.Et ſi nec hic cauſe nutritionis ſufficientes appareant, ad locum con iunctionis vel oppoſitionis immediate natiuitatem præcedentis conſugiunt. Denique ad 4 & & aſpiciunt, quorum vnum Gin angulis vel ſuccedentibus à malis & impedimétis liberum deprehendant, nato nutritionem promittunt. Enunciati huius duo funt membra. Primum eſt de inueniendo tempore nutritionis,an videlicet illi qui naſcitur, concedatur à calo vt nutriatur vel non nu triatur. Secundum eſt de ijs, quæ cognito tempore nutritionis quæruntur,quçq; ad vniuerſam vita à ſtellis decretam pertinent. Nam cùm aſcendens noſtræ vitæ fit principium, principium quoque educationis non abſurdè ad eiuſdem triangularitatis dominos refer tur. Quæ verò ad reliquam vitam totam, ſi longior nato concedatur, pertinent, vt ſcire poſsis, trigonocratoras loci luminaris conditionarij ſimiliter contemplari oportet. Vocatur au tem luminare aigale feu conditionarium illud, quod temporis conditionem ſequitur. Sicut enim Sol in die, ita Luna in note potentior & efficacior exiſtit. Itaque ſi matiuitas fuerit diurna, triangulatores loci Solis, ſi nocturna, triangulatores loci Lunæ aſpiciédierút:quifi omnes fuerint fortunati, fortes, recepti, in bonis locis & dignitatibus fuis, decernuntnato pro- ſperitaté & diuitias in omnibus diebus vitæ eius. translation: Nocturnal nativity with Scorpio ascending. If one was fortunate, but the others unfortunate, the fortunate one promises substance and riches to the native during the age distributed to him; the unfortunate ones, however, threaten calamity and indigence during the age divided to them. The first triplicity ruler signifies the beginning of life, or rather the first third part of a person's life; the second administers the second; and finally, the third administers the third third of a person's life. As in the following nocturnal nativity, where the Part of Fortune falls in the horoscope at 2 degrees, 30 minutes of Libra, and the positions of the planets are as you see, the status of life is signified by the triplicity rulers of the Moon's place traversing the sign of Cancer, whose first triplicity ruler was Mars, the second Jupiter, both cadent in the sixth and third houses, and thus they threaten a bad state of life for the native. But the Moon, which was the luminary of the time, placed in the midheaven, and moreover the last lord of the triplicity, and of the nature of Venus, decrees beauty and good fortune for the native at the end of life. For when the triplicity rulers of the conditioned luminary are from angular houses...

translation: From these examples, a clear disagreement between Ptolemy and the Arabs is evident regarding three major points. First, in the structure and distribution of the heavens. For the Arabs, dividing the zodiac into twelve equal parts, follow an equal method. On the contrary, Ptolemy divides not the zodiac, but the equinoctial into equal dodecatemoria, and with respect to the zodiac, he creates plainly unequal domiciles, as has been extensively explained above. Secondly, it is clear that the Arabs, for determining the place of the Part of Fortune in diurnal nativities, cast it from the Sun to the Moon, but in nocturnal nativities, they cast it from the distance from the ascendant, contrary to what Ptolemy does. Ptolemy universally prescribes that in both diurnal and nocturnal nativities, the distance from the Sun to the Moon should be taken, and that it should be counted from the ascendant, so that the end of the counting shows the place of the Part of Fortune. In the preceding example, since it is a nocturnal nativity, the place of the Part of Fortune, according to the opinion of the Arabs, is noted at the fifth part of Taurus, while according to Ptolemy's calculation, it falls at the beginning of Aquarius. Thirdly, it appears from a comparison of the precepts that the Arabs derive indications of effects from lighter causes, while Ptolemy derives them from more potent and stronger causes. For example, here, where the danger of nourishment is questioned, Ptolemy considers the principal causes of life, namely the affliction of the luminaries and cardinal points by malefic stars. The Arabs, on the contrary, do not so much consider the luminaries themselves, as the condition of the luminaries and the relationship of the triangulators of the ascendant to the angles. Similarly, we will find that Ptolemy considered only the more potent and stronger causes for all other effects, omitting lighter conjectures. Indeed, he not only omits these idle questions and empty lots, but also often severely criticizes them. From what we have said about the first house, it is easy to judge and understand the author's narration concerning the other houses. For the triangulators of the other houses and their significations are judged no differently than those of the first house. For example, in marriage, which they attribute to the seventh house, the triangulators of the seventh place and Venus are considered; in journeys, however, the triangulators of the ninth house and the place of Mars; and similarly in other matters, other triangulators are considered, as is obvious to anyone in the books of the Arabs.

III. fignificant rem apertam & detectam cum fama, XII. VI.fignificant occultationem & reten tionem & vilitatem rerum: Anguli autem & dommi angulorum fignificant magnitudinem honorum, precij atque fortune, fortitudinem & exercitationem Ge longationem à cafu: Et prafentia eorum in cadentibus eft contraria fortuna, id est, dedecus & cafus. De fuccε dentibus verò angulorum domus quæ fuccedit decima, id est, vndecima fignificat fortitudinem & fortunam mediam amicorum,& ex ea parte in qua erat fiducia: Et illa que ſequitur quartam, id est, quinta, ſignificat fortunam mediam per donationes & venerationes, & caufa filiorum cum veneratione, letitia & gaudio: que autem fuccedit afcendenti, que est fecunda, ſignificat, militer fortunam mediam ex caufa cauſa ſubſtantie & mini strorum: quæ verò fuccedit ſeptima, id est, octaua figni ficat fimiliter fortunam mediamex Almauerith, id est, ex subftantia que hæreditatur à mortuis & rebus occultis. fo Ho Digitized by Google ==End of OCR for page 4== ==Start of OCR for page 5== 2 : 1 : ۴: 1 PRIMA. translation: The twelve houses also signify colors, and these are they: For the ascending or first and seventh houses are white; the second and twelfth are green; the third and eleventh are saffron; the fourth and tenth are red; the fifth and ninth are honey-colored, that is, they have the color of honey; the sixth and eighth are black.

title: Joys of the Planets in Houses

Primò in ſtrutura & diſtribu tione cæli. Illi enim ſigniferum in duodecim partes æquales dirimentes, modum æqualem ſequuntur. translation: And each planet has a certain power in each of these houses, from the accidental powers, which is called joy. Because Mercury rejoices in the ascendant, the Moon in the third house, Venus also in the fifth, Mars in the sixth, the Sun in the ninth, Jupiter in the eleventh, Saturn in the twelfth. Here, he also assigns colors to the houses of the sky. Just as Saturn agrees particularly with blackness, Jupiter and Venus with whiteness, Mars with redness, etc., according to their temperament; so also he assigns whiteness to the horizon, and thus consequently always two celestial stations equally distant from the horizon to one color, namely the second and twelfth to green, the third and eleventh to saffron, the fourth and tenth to red, the fifth and ninth to honey-colored, the sixth and eighth to black. They say that knowledge of this is useful for knowing which colors of clothes, horses, and such things will be fortunate or unfortunate for the native. For cusps illuminated by benefics and free from malefics declare fortunate colors, but damaged ones declare unfortunate colors. But Ptolemy never mentions this in his Apotelesmatics. However, what he says, that each of the planets has a certain accidental power in some of the said houses, which is called joy, can be excused by reasons and deduced from Ptolemy's sayings. For first, since Mercury is the significator of the mind, which most strongly affects the mind in the ascendant, it is rightly said to rejoice in the ascendant. Then, since the third house is of short journeys, the Moon, which is itself wandering and unstable, will not absurdly be considered familiar, although for this reason it seems more appropriate to the ninth and especially the seventh: for placed there, it wonderfully incites the native's mind to undertake journeys. The fifth house loves Venus, the second and prolific star, because from this place alone the Arabs primarily infer the number and quality of children. Ptolemy also does not neglect this in this matter, but attributes more important parts to the tenth and eleventh. The sixth place is that it is the place of health and misfortune. The ninth place is attributed to the Sun, because both signify religion. The eleventh house signifies fortune, and is called by Ptolemy agathodaimon or agathopoios daimon: therefore, Jupiter is attributed to it, just as the twelfth, which is unfortunate, and for that reason is called by Ptolemy kakodaimon or kakos daimon.

Etenim quælibet domus ex p.30.conftat,nempe partibus 5.ante initium domus, & p.25. proximè cuſpidem ſequentibus, idque ſi Ptolemæum ſequamur,de æquinoctialis circuli partibus potifsimè intelligendum eft, vt fuprà diximus. DE SIGNIFICATIONE DOMInorum angulorum. Ha quoque sunt fignificationes dominorum angula rum dum fuerint præsentes in angulis. Præsentia domini aſcendentis in aſcendente, signifi cat eius fortunam per ſemetipsum & per eius acquiſitio- $ nem:& fignificant preſen
translation: And it is said in the signification of the houses that the angles signify strength and perfection, while those falling from the angles signify weakness and detriment, except that the ninth and third signify an open and revealed matter with fame, while the twelfth and sixth signify concealment and retention and worthlessness of things. But the angles and the lords of the angles signify greatness of honors, value, and fortune, strength and exercise, and prolongation from fall. And their presence in cadent houses is contrary to fortune, that is, disgrace and fall. Concerning the succedent houses of the angles, the house that succeeds the tenth, that is, the eleventh, signifies strength and moderate fortune of friends, and from that part in which there was trust. And that which follows the fourth, that is, the fifth, signifies moderate fortune through gifts and venerations, and the cause of children with veneration, joy, and gladness. But that which succeeds the ascendant, which is the second, similarly signifies moderate fortune from the cause of substance and servants. But that which succeeds the seventh, that is, the eighth, similarly signifies moderate fortune from Almauerith, that is, from substance inherited from the dead and hidden things.

translation: This entire chapter can be understood from what was said a little earlier about the strengths of the celestial stations. The angles are the strongest, to which the succedent houses approach most closely in strength. However, the cadent houses signify weakness and detriment, except that, as the author says, the ninth house with the third signifies an open and revealed matter with fame: for they are, as he said a little earlier, the joys of the luminaries. Similarly, the twelfth and sixth are said to signify concealment and worthlessness of things, because they are the joys of the infortunes. Therefore, he makes three states of strength in the houses: the angles are the strongest; the places succeeding the angles have moderate strength; and the cadent places are entirely devoid of strength. Among these primary states, there are infinite intermediate ones, the judgment of which is left to the sagacity of the prudent Astrologer. Some have tried to explain the variety of states in individual houses by this kind of similitude. A planet in the first part of an angle or even another house has its total power, which is like 8. But when it is distant from the cusp by 5 parts, it loses one power; if it is distant by 10 parts, it loses two; if by 15 parts, it loses 3; if by 20, it loses 4; if by 25, it loses 6. Furthermore, the remaining 5 degrees partake of the nature of the following house. For each house consists of 30 parts, namely 5 parts before the beginning of the house, and 25 parts immediately following the cusp, and this, if we follow Ptolemy, is to be understood primarily of the parts of the equinoctial circle, as we said above.

translation: These are also the significations of the lords of the angles when they are present in the angles. The presence of the lord of the ascendant in the ascendant signifies his fortune through himself and through his acquisition: and they signify the presence.

author: Valentin Naibod
title: Lord of the Tenth House
text: tia eius in decima per regem magisteria altiora: cum fuerit in ſeptima, per con uentiones factores atque vxores:per prafentia quoque eius in quarta, fignificat fortunam per hereditates & A caufas Digitized by Google ==End of OCR for page 5== 188 DIFFEREΝΤΙΑ cauſas patrum, & per productiones aquarum & po pulationes vel plantationes, id eft, adificationes, ex rebus antiquis & radicalibus. Significat quoque dominus domus decima per pras fentiam fuar iam fuam in eadem decimafortunam vel per regem vel per regnum magnum & magiſteria altiora:& figni ficat per præſentiam ſuam in ſeptima regnum per victo. riam contentionum ex caufis vxorum: & per pra. ſentiam eius in quarta fignat regnum per amicitiam no bilium regis eius, & per caufas tribunorum, & per difpenfatores regum,& cultus terrarum, & edificatn nes ciuitatum, & per diuifiones fluminum, & cuftodias ciuitatum,& ex rebus antiquis: & per præſentiam fu am in afcendente fignificat regnum per ingenia & per propinquitatem regis rebus vulgi. Preſentia autem dominiſeptime inſeptima ſignifie cat fortunam per negotiationes & per conuentiones: per nutritiones quoque & mulieres, & per ſatores: fignificat per præſentiam eius in quarta, per conuentiones mulierum & per negotiationes, & per caufas pa trum hereditatum & cultus terre, prafentia fua in afcendente per cõuentiones & negotiationes, per cas Jas medicinæ atque astronomie, & per opera ſpiritualia genia, & confimilia: & fignificat per præſentiam fuam in decima fortunam per conuentiones nego tiationes, & per vxores & per caufas regis. Præfentia verò domini quarte in quarta ſignificat fortunam ex cultu terre ofructu, per caufas patru : atque res PRIMA at que res antiquas: per preſentiam eius in afcenden te fignificat fortunam ex cultu terra & fructu per inge nium & confilij profunditatem : & præfentia fua in de cima fignificat profectum ex cultu terra & fructu per causas regis & magiſteriorum. Et præſentia fua in ſepti ma fignificat fortunam ex cultu terre & fructuum per causas vxorum, & fororum & per negotiationes. Hec fignificant domini angulorum per prafentiam fuam in angulis fuis. Similiter ergo facies de præfentia dominorum & ceterarum domorum, & nos ideo intro. duximus tantùm dominos angulorum, vt effent exem plar in ceteris. In hoc capite decreta dominorum angulorum ſi ijdem præ ſentesin ipſis angulis reperiatur, plano & prolixo fermonere. citat, vt hoc veluti exemplo doceat, vniuerfaliter rerum guber natores feu ſignificatores, vt loquuntur, decernere non tantùm fecundum propriam fuam naturam, fed etiam fecundum pro. prietatatem omnium ſtationum cæli in quibus reperiuntur. Maioris euidentiæ gratia repetatur vnum ex exemplis autoris. Is dicit, dominum quartæ ſi ſit potens, fignificare fortunam ex agricultura & reliquis terre fructibus. lă verò ſi vlteri quę- ratur, qua occaſione ſic natus copiam agrorum & talem fortuné fit confecuturus:reſpondet, id ex natura domus in qua dictus fignificator, hoc eſt, dominus quartæ domus reperitur, eſſe coijciendum.Nam fi præſens reperiatur in ipſa quarta domo,quæ eſt patrum, ſignificatur opes tales acceffuras ex hæreditate aut alia occaſione patrum aut rerum antiquarum: & ſi ſit in domo prima, quæ nati corpus habet atque animum, ſignificatur talia bona ventura per nati proprium ingeniuminduſtriamque: & fi reperiatur in decima, quæ eſt regum atque magiſterij ſeu actionum,ex quibus in communi ſocietate homines victum quæritant, fignificatur poſſeſsio agrorum occafione regis & magiſterij:ſi denique reperiatur in domo ſeptima, que eft vxorum'atq; focie- Ла 3 190 DIFFERENΤΙΑ ſocietatum,conijciendum eſt prædia natum confecuturum ab vxoribus vel negotiatione, & talibus occaſionibus quæ proprie tatibus ſeptima domus congruunt. Simili modo de reliquis fi gnificatoribus omnibus iudicandum eſt. QUALITER SCIATVR QVIS planeta fit dominator. .. Et cùm volueris ſcire planetam dominatorem rei, afpicias quis planetarum fit maioris autoritatis in do. mo rei, & planetam qui fignificat naturam illius rei, ficut dicemus in naturis planetarum. Vide ergo, quis pla netarum fit fortior in domo rei, & in parte eius, ex fortitudinibus quas prædiximus, id eft, ex numero quem prædiximus, dum de fortitudine planetarum potesta te tractaremus. Et qui fortior omnibus fuerit in loco rei, ipfe erit dominator eius. Verbi gratia, fiinterrogatio fuerit de ſubſtantia, & volueris ſcire quis fit dominator eius, &fuerit ſecunda domus, quæ fignificat ſubſtatiam, ſcilicet quintus gradus ſigni Arietis : quia domus eft Martis, babet in hoc loco Mars quinque fortitudines: exaltatio quoque eft Solis, & habet in eo quatuor fortis tudines. Estetiam ipſius Solis triplicitas, & habet in ineo eo tres fortitudines, habet ergo Solibi ſeptem fortitudi nes. Et est terminus Iouis, & habet ibi duas fortitudi nes. Et eft etiam facies Martis, & habet ibi vnamfor titudinem. Ergo Mars habet ibi fex fortitudines, quinque ex domo, Tunam ex facie, Sol ſeptem, &talis planeta vocatur Almubta, id eft, vincens. Sol ergo acci pit ibi principatum, quia habet ibi ſeptem fortitudines, Gipfe PREMA &ipfe dominatur ibi in domo ſubſtantie. Similiter aca cipies ibi in loco partis ſubſtantie & partis fortune, participem illorum facies Iouem, qui eſt ſignificator ſubſtantia naturaliter, commiſcebis partium 15 pla netarum teftimonia. Et facies hoc idem de omnibus do. mibus, & fcies dominatoremearum. In preſenticapite tradit modum inueniendi planetas rerum dominatores, hoc eſt, omnem maioremúe ceteris poteftatem decernendi alicuius rei euentus habentes. Hinc vocabula queda exponenda ſunt. Differunt dominus hoſpitatorq;, & dominator ſeu gubernator loci, quamuis idem planeta fimul & dominator & dominus & etiam hofpitator eſſe inueniatur.Dominus ſigni gręcè xue, & nonnungetiam οἰκοδεσπότης dicitur, atque qui in eodem ſigno domicilium habet. Veluti ſigni Arie tis dominus eft, & Tauri domina eſt &, & Geminorum do minus eft &, &c. Quapropter fi in cardinem exorientem incidat ſignum Arietis, dominus domus prime erit, ſecundef, tertię ,quarte ( &c. fiquidem oriente Ariete ſignum Tauri in locum fecundum,& II in locum tertium, & in locu quar tum iuxta diſtributionem cæli equalem Arabibus approbată, incidet. Firmicus dominum ſigni hofpitatorem, nonnunquam etiam dominatorem vocat. Quo pacto quando hoſpitator interlunij nominatur, dominus loci in quo & facta eſt, intelligitur. Dicitur etiam & magis propriè planeta alterius planete hoſpitator & tanquam paterfamilias, cum illum in do mum fuam recipiens eius curam patrociniumq; hoſpitij iure ſuſcipit. Veluti fi & in my fit conftituta, Mercurium Veneris hoſpitatorem, quòd my mercurij domus fit, dicimus. Sic ſi Sol fit in Ariete, tunc Solis patrocinium iure hofpitii Mars perinde vt paterfamilias propria in domo accipit. Sed dominator ſeu gubernator diſpoſitorque loci Arabicè Almutes, & gręce pla neta ἐπικρατήτωρ,quod in figno ſignite gradu epicrateſin, id eſt, dominatum potiorefq; prerogatiuas inter alias planetas ob tineat, dicitur. Verbi gratia, in genitura diurnaaſcendit par.6. Venus ibidem habet poteſtates quinque ratione ſui domicilij.
translation: Its presence in the tenth house signifies higher offices through the king; when it is in the seventh, through conventions, agents, and wives; its presence in the fourth also signifies fortune through inheritances and the affairs of fathers, and through the production of waters and populations or plantations, that is, buildings, from ancient and radical things. The lord of the tenth house also signifies, through its presence in the same tenth house, fortune either through the king or through a great kingdom and higher offices; and through its presence in the seventh, it signifies a kingdom through victory in contentions arising from the affairs of wives; and through its presence in the fourth, it signifies a kingdom through the friendship of the king's nobles, and through the affairs of tribunes, and through the king's dispensers, and the cultivation of lands, and the building of cities, and through the division of rivers, and the guardianship of cities, and from ancient things; and through its presence in the ascendant, it signifies a kingdom through ingenuity and through the king's proximity to the affairs of the common people. The presence of the lord of the seventh in the seventh signifies fortune through negotiations and conventions; also through nourishment and women, and through agents; its presence in the fourth signifies through conventions of women and through negotiations, and through the affairs of fathers, inheritances, and cultivation of the land; its presence in the ascendant through conventions and negotiations, through the affairs of medicine and astronomy, and through spiritual works, geniuses, and similar things; and its presence in the tenth signifies fortune through conventions, negotiations, and through wives and through the king's affairs. The presence of the lord of the fourth in the fourth signifies fortune from the cultivation of the land and its fruit, through the affairs of fathers, and ancient things; its presence in the ascendant signifies fortune from the cultivation of the land and its fruit through ingenuity and depth of counsel; and its presence in the tenth signifies profit from the cultivation of the land and its fruit through the affairs of the king and magistrates. And its presence in the seventh signifies fortune from the cultivation of the land and its fruits through the affairs of wives, and sisters, and through negotiations. These are what the lords of the angles signify by their presence in their own angles. You will do similarly for the presence of the lords of the other houses, and we have therefore introduced only the lords of the angles, so that they might be an example for the others. In this chapter, the decrees of the lords of the angles, if they are found present in those very angles, are recited in plain and extensive discourse, so that this may teach, as it were by example, that the universal governors or significators of things, as they say, are to be determined not only according to their own nature, but also according to the property of all the celestial stations in which they are found. For greater clarity, let one of the author's examples be repeated. He says that the lord of the fourth, if it is powerful, signifies fortune from agriculture and the other fruits of the earth. Now, if it is further asked by what occasion a native thus born will obtain an abundance of fields and such fortune, he answers that this is to be inferred from the nature of the house in which the said significator, that is, the lord of the fourth house, is found. For if it is found present in the fourth house itself, which is that of fathers, such wealth is signified to accrue from inheritance or another occasion of fathers or ancient things; and if it is in the first house, which contains the native's body and soul, such goods are signified to come through the native's own ingenuity and industry; and if it is found in the tenth, which is that of kings and magistracy or actions, from which men seek a living in common society, the possession of fields is signified by occasion of the king and magistracy; finally, if it is found in the seventh house, which is that of wives and partnerships, it is to be inferred that the native will obtain estates from wives or negotiation, and such occasions as are congruent with the properties of the seventh house. In a similar way, judgment is to be made concerning all other significators.

author: Valentin Naibod
title: How to Find the Dominant Planet

translation: And when you wish to know the dominant planet of a thing, look at which of the planets has the greatest authority in the house of the thing, and the planet that signifies the nature of that thing, as we will say in the natures of the planets. Therefore, see which of the planets is stronger in the house of the thing, and in its part, from the strengths we have already mentioned, that is, from the number we have already mentioned, when we were discussing the strength and power of the planets. And whoever is stronger than all in the place of the thing, he will be its dominator. For example, if the question is about substance, and you wish to know who is its dominator, and the second house, which signifies substance, is, for example, the fifth degree of the sign of Aries: because the house is Mars', Mars has five strengths in this place; the exaltation is also of the Sun, and it has four strengths there. It is also the Sun's triplicity, and it has three strengths there, so the Sun has seven strengths there. And it is the term of Jupiter, and it has two strengths there. And it is also the face of Mars, and it has one strength there. Therefore, Mars has six strengths there, five from the house, one from the face, the Sun seven, and such a planet is called Almubta, that is, conquering. Therefore, the Sun takes the principality there, because it has seven strengths there, if it itself dominates there in the house of substance. Similarly, you will take Jupiter as a participant there in the place of the part of substance and the part of fortune, which is naturally the significator of substance, you will mix the testimonies of the 15 parts of the planets. And you will do this for all houses, and you will know their dominator. In the present chapter, it teaches the method of finding the dominant planets of things, that is, those having greater power than others in determining the outcome of any matter. Hence, some terms need to be explained. The lord and the host differ, as do the dominator or governor of the place, although the same planet may be found to be simultaneously dominator, lord, and also host. The lord of a sign is called in Greek κυριος, and sometimes also οἰκοδεσπότης, and is he who has his domicile in the same sign. For example, the lord of the sign of Aries is Mars, and the lady of Taurus is Venus, and the lord of Gemini is Mercury, etc. Therefore, if the sign of Aries falls into the rising angle, Mars will be the lord of the first house, Venus of the second, Mercury of the third, Jupiter of the fourth (etc., if Aries is rising, the sign of Taurus will fall into the second place, and Gemini into the third place, and Cancer into the fourth place according to the equal distribution of the sky approved by the Arabs). Firmicus calls the lord of the sign a host, and sometimes also a dominator. In this way, when the host of the interlunium is named, the lord of the place in which the interlunium occurred is understood. A planet is also more properly called the host of another planet and, as it were, the head of the household, when receiving it into its house, it undertakes its care and patronage by right of hospitality. For example, if Mercury is constituted in Taurus, we say that Mercury is the host of Venus, because Taurus is the house of Venus. Similarly, if the Sun is in Aries, then Mars, as the head of the household, receives the patronage of the Sun by right of hospitality in its own house. But the dominator or governor and disposer of the place is called in Arabic Almutes, and in Greek planeta επικρατήτωρ, because in the sign and degree of the sign it obtains epicratesin, that is, dominion and superior prerogatives among other planets. For example, in a diurnal nativity, the 6th part ascends. Venus has five powers there by reason of its domicile.

author: Valentin Naibod
title: Significations of Signs and Planets
text: 78 DIFFERENTIA & X. Argo verò vtriufq; naturæ eft. Alia terreſtria χερία, &horum alia hominum nominibus vocata ἀνθρωπόμορφα, que nota funt:alia bruta ἀλόγα. Rurfus brutorum alia funt quadru pedia τετράποδα, quæ vt dictum eſt in fera ανήμερα, & domeſticaἅμερα diſtrahuntur: alia reptilia gausing, vt ferpentes & fimilia: Alia venenoſaΐοβολα, vt Scorpio & ferpentes: alia mati lata:alia vincta:alia aliter denominata, DE SIGNIFICATIONIBVS SIGNORVM. Et vnumquodque fignumhabet propriam ſignifica tionem in his, quæ fignificant ex creatione membrorum moribus hominum, & regionum & ſeminum, & are borum catera. Aries habet ex corpore hominis ca put & faciem : & ex regionibus bebil & ferim, id eft, Babyloniam & Arabiam & Falastim & Palestinam. Taurus habet arbores que plantantur:& ex corpore hominis habet collwn 15 gutturis nodum:& ex regioni bus Ezetimet & Almech & Sandem & Araterad. Gemini eft fignum largum, boni animi, habet ex corpore hominis humeros & brachia & manus: Gex regionibus Vrgeti & Armeniam, Nuthrabigem Etele nimſtet, id eft, Aegyptum & Bartha. Cancer habet ex arboribus eas que fuerint aquales longitudine,& ex corpore hominis pectus, cor, ſtomachum, coſtas, fple nes & pulmonem: & ex regionibus Armeniam mino. rem,& Orientalem plagam Buthabem & Maſſem Acin:15 habet participatione in Barach & Atrabicen. Leo habet arbores proceras, id eft, longas, callidus verſutus, & multe anguftice & triſtitie: & habet ex corpo. Virgo habet quicquid ſeminatur ex feminibus, & est larga, boni animi. Et habet ex corpore hominis ventrem interiora fcilicet telam, id eft, διάφραγμα & intesti= na:15 ex regionibus argumenta et affem, id eft,quædam regio circa Hierufalem, & Alforas,id eft, Eufraten: infulam que Hifpania & Festix vel Afeum. Libra habet arbores proceras, & eſt larga, boni animi. Et habet ex corpore hominis lumbos & inferiora ventris, vmbilicu & pectinem & verenda, anchas & ilia & na. tes:Et ex regionibus terram Romanorum vel Grecorum, & quæ fuccedunt eis affines vsque ad Africam: habet etiam Araffe vel Azim vfque in finem Aethiopie Barthan: habet & Carmen & Fegeſtem Thebis vel Thebil Tabraſten Barah Ambarath. Scors pius habet arbores longitudine æquales, largus, boni ani mi:Et habet ex hominis corpore verenda, testiculos, ve ficamanum fæmora:t ex regionibus Helehiget rura Arabum fines eius vsque in Allegem: & habet in Alchachparticipationem. Sagittarius ingeniofus callidus, habet ex corpore hominis fæmora:& ex re gionibus Aethiopia & Maharahen & Achiut vel Ace nit vel Achichit, que est, India. Capricornus bonæ vitæ, iracundus, cautus & callidus, multe triftitie: Habens ex corpore hominis genua: Etex regionibus Aethio. piam, Annaban, Alcon & Alcuth, Mahamen vfq; ad duo maria Aetahante Achiut, que est India. Aquas rius. translation: 78. Argo is of both natures. Others are terrestrial, and of these, some are called anthropomorphic, which are known; others are irrational. Again, of the irrational, some are quadrupeds, which, as has been said, are divided into wild and domestic; others are reptiles, like serpents and similar creatures; others are venomous, like the Scorpion and serpents; others are mutilated; others are bound; others are otherwise named. CONCERNING THE SIGNIFICATIONS OF THE SIGNS. And each sign has its own signification in those things which it signifies from the creation of the members, the customs of men, and regions, and seeds, and trees, etc. Aries has from the human body the head and face; and from regions, Bebil and Serim, that is, Babylon and Arabia and Falastim and Palestine. Taurus has trees that are planted; and from the human body it has the neck and the throat node; and from regions, Ezetimet and Almech and Sandem and Araterad. Gemini is a generous sign, of good spirit, it has from the human body the shoulders and arms and hands; and from regions, Urgeti and Armenia, Nuthrabigem Etele nimstet, that is, Egypt and Bartha. Cancer has from trees those that are equal in length, and from the human body the chest, heart, stomach, ribs, spleen, and lung; and from regions, Lesser Armenia, and the Eastern region Buthabem and Massem Acin; it has participation in Barach and Atrabicen. Leo has tall trees, that is, long ones, cunning, astute, and much distress and sadness; and it has from the human body the stomach, heart, back, side; from regions, Artitri up to the end of the habitable region. Virgo has whatever is sown from seeds, and is generous, of good spirit. And it has from the human body the belly, namely the internal membrane, that is, the diaphragm and intestines; from regions, arguments and assent, that is, a certain region around Jerusalem, and Alforas, that is, the Euphrates; the island of Spain and Festix or Aseum. Libra has tall trees, and is generous, of good spirit. And it has from the human body the loins and lower belly, navel and groin and genitals, hips and flanks and buttocks; and from regions, the land of the Romans or Greeks, and those adjacent to them up to Africa; it also has Araffe or Azim up to the end of Ethiopia Barthan; it also has Carmen and Fegestem Thebes or Thebil Tabrasten Barah Ambarath. Scorpio has trees of equal length, generous, of good spirit; and it has from the human body the genitals, testicles, bladder, thighs; and from regions, Helehiget, the rural areas of the Arabs, its borders up to Allegem; and it has participation in Alchach. Sagittarius is ingenious, cunning, has from the human body the thighs; and from regions, Ethiopia and Maharahen and Achiut or Acenit or Achichit, which is India. Capricorn is of good life, irascible, cautious and cunning, much sadness; having from the human body the knees; and from regions, Ethiopia, Annaban, Alcon and Alcuth, Mahamen up to the two seas Aetahante Achiut, which is India. Aquarius has from the human body the legs up to the lower ankles, that is, the talus; and from regions, Azenes, that is, blackness, and Althupha and its parts, and the land of Elfices, and part of the land of Egypt, and the Western region of the land of Achiut. Pisces, cunning and cautious, mixed, multi-colored, having from the human body the feet; and from regions, Carabrarem and the Northern region of the land of Jurgem, and participation in the Romans up to Effen, it has the island and Egypt, and Alexandria and the Lycian Sea. All things enclosed beneath the lunar heaven are subject to the superior stars and celestial signs, and they agree with their powers, sympathize, and are moved around more manifestly than can be persuaded by verbose proof. For we note in this lower and sublunar world certain solar bodies, such as gold among metals, hyacinth among stones, heliotrope and lupine among plants, and among these the phoenix, etc., to which the Sun communicates something of its virtue more than to others: Certain lunar things, such as silver, crystal, palm, hyssop, geese, seals, and such things, which are especially nourished and preserved and affected more evidently by the Moon. In the same way, we must think and judge about Saturnine, Jovial, Venereal, Mercurial things, and finally about each celestial sign particularly agreeing. Therefore, just as the author above explained the places of the heaven and the stars, so in this chapter he intends to narrate the sympathy of these same places of the heaven with certain inferior things and those adjacent to the earth, that is, he wants to show which places of the heaven or dodecatemoria, to which regions, provinces, cities, trees, plants, parts of the human body, and such things especially agree and consent, and to which they attribute their power more than to others: all of which are so plainly narrated here that they require no explanation, except that a large part of the places are obscured by barbarous and unknown words. Therefore, I will add from Ptolemy which nations are placed under which signs, to whose brief catalog other places observed by younger authors may be added. These are to be observed because all the significations of the heaven appear more in those places which are subjected to the parts of the heaven decreeing something. Therefore, Aries has Britain, Brachate Gaul, Germany, the Bastarnae, Coele-Syria, Palestine, Idumea, Judea. Taurus has Parthia, central Persia, the Cyclades islands, the island of Cyprus, maritime places of Asia Minor. Gemini has Hyrcania, Armenia, Martiana, Cyrene, Marmarica, lower Egypt. Cancer has the Numidians, Carthaginians, Africans, Bithynia, Phrygia, Colchis. Leo has Italy, Togate Gaul, Sicily, Apulia, the Phoenicians, Chaldeans, Ochiniam. Virgo has Mesopotamia, Babylon, Assyria, Greece, Achaia, Crete. Libra has Bactra, Casperia, the Seres, Thebaid, Oasis, the Troglodytes. Scorpio has Metagonitida, the Moors, Gaetulians, Syria, Commagene, the Cappadocians. Sagittarius has the Tyrrhenians, Celts, Spain, happy Arabia. Capricorn has India, Arrianen, Gedrosia, Thrace, the Macedonians, Illyricum. Aquarius has the Sauromatae, Oxianen, Sogdianen, Arabia, Azania, central Ethiopia. Pisces has Phazaia, Nazamonitis, Garamantice, Lydia, Cilicia, Pamphylia. This is the sympathy of the twelve signs with the regions. Now we will also describe which parts of the human body the same signs hold, for this is very beneficial for judgments, especially when we want to find the place of health or defect. MEMBERS OF THE SIGNS. Head, neck, shoulders and arms, chest from the collarbone, belly from the breastbone to the lower parts, buttocks, genitals, bladder, hips, knees, legs and shins, feet, thighs. Refer here to the 20th statement of the Centiloquium, by which surgeons are warned not to cut veins or excise tumors when that sign of the heaven passes through to which the member to be cut is attributed. That is, do not strike a member with iron when the Moon holds the sign which rules that member. For the Moon, mistress of humors, is accustomed to fill that member which is subject to the sign through which she passes, more than others, with humors. Therefore, if it is opened with iron at that time, from the excessive influx of humors, putrid ulcers, nerve distensions, and such evils can follow, which both injure the member and make the cure dangerous. From this it is also clear why those wounds which are inflicted when the Moon illuminates the sign which rules the wounded part are healed with more difficulty and scarcely without harm to the member. And these two, namely the sympathy or consent of the parts of the earth and the members of the human body with the places of the heaven, are the main things which we are bound to observe in this chapter. CONCERNING THE SIGNIFICATION OF THE PLANETS IN THE SIGNS ACCORDING TO THE MEMBERS OF MAN. And if indeed the planets signify pain, they have in each sign a member proper to them. Therefore, let us treat of the pains of the planets in the signs. And first let us begin with Aries. Saturn in Aries has the chest, Jupiter the belly, Mars the head, Sun the thighs, Venus the feet, Mercury the legs, Moon the knees. In Taurus, Saturn the belly, Jupiter the back, Mars the neck, Sun the knees, Venus the head, Mercury the feet, Moon the legs. In Gemini, Saturn the belly, Jupiter the genitals.

author: Valentin Naibod
title: Fixed Stars in Virgo, Libra, Scorpio

translation: The length of the preceding star is 161 degrees, 10 minutes, with a northern latitude of 25 degrees. The length of the following star, in the shape of an ivy leaf, is 169 degrees, 10 minutes, 13 seconds, with a northern latitude of 25 degrees, 30 minutes, 12 seconds. The winged star of Virgo is depicted in the starry globes. The stars that are in the head of Virgo, and one at the extremity of the southern wing, imitate the nature of Mercury and somewhat of Mars. The other bright stars of the same wing, and those in the girdle, follow the nature of Mercury and a little of Venus. The other northern wing is marked by two famous stars. First, there is found in it a bright star of the third magnitude, which our people call Vindemiator, the Greeks call it Protrugetes, and in Chaldean, Almucedie Alaraph. Its longitude is 182 degrees, 50 minutes, 12 seconds. Its northern latitude is 15 degrees, 10 minutes, 13 seconds. Then another star of the same wing, of the first magnitude, which our people call Spica Virginis, the Greeks call it Stachys, and the Chaldeans Alzimon Alazel, has a longitude of 197 degrees, 20 minutes, 13 seconds, that is, it belongs to the 17th degree, 20 minutes, 18 seconds of the dodecatemory of Libra, with a southern latitude of 2 degrees. Its nature is that of Venus and somewhat of Mars. Finally, the stars that are at the ends of the feet and in the hem of the garment have the same effect as Mercury and Mars, and somewhat of Venus. The asterism of the Claws (Libra) is figured by 8 stars, of which two are of the second magnitude, four of the fourth, and two of the fifth. Their nature, described by Ptolemy, is in these words: "Those in the extremities of the claws are disposed similarly to Jupiter and Venus. Those in the middle are like Saturn, and gently like Mercury." In the forehead of Scorpio, there are three bright stars of the third magnitude, of the nature of Mars and somewhat of Saturn. The longitude of the northernmost is 237 degrees, that is, it is in the 27th degree of the dodecatemory of Scorpio, with a northern latitude of 1 degree, 20 minutes, 1 second. The longitude of the middle star is 236 degrees, 20 minutes, 18 seconds, with a southern latitude of 1 degree, 40 minutes, 12 seconds. The longitude of the southernmost is likewise 236 degrees, 20 minutes, 1 second, with a southern latitude of 5 degrees. Three bright stars in the body are of the nature of Mars and somewhat of Jupiter. But the preceding of these is of the third magnitude, and has a longitude of 241 degrees, 20 minutes, 1 second, with a southern latitude of 3 degrees, 45 minutes, 12 seconds. The middle one, bright and reddish, called Cor Scorpii by moderns, and Antares by the Greeks, is of the second magnitude, falling in the 3rd degree, 20 minutes of Sagittarius, with a southern latitude of 4 degrees. The following of the three is of the third magnitude, and has a longitude of 245 degrees, 10 minutes, 1 second, with a southern latitude of 5 degrees, 1 minute. Of those in the vertebrae, most are of the third magnitude, imitating the nature of Saturn and a little of Venus. The one in the first vertebra falls in the 9th degree of Sagittarius, with a southern latitude of 11 degrees. The one in the seventh vertebra and closest to the sting is found in the 19th degree, 40 minutes of Sagittarius, with a southern latitude of 15 degrees. Of the two in the sting, the preceding is of the fourth magnitude, and has a longitude of 257 degrees, with a southern latitude of 13 degrees. The following is of the third magnitude, and has a longitude of 258 degrees, with a southern latitude of 13 degrees. They have the same effect as Mars and Saturn. The so-called nebulous cluster, that is, the nebulous circle around Scorpio, is of the nature of Mars and the Moon, and belongs to the 21st degree, 50 minutes, 12 seconds of Sagittarius, with a southern latitude of 13 degrees. The arrow of Sagittarius is Martian and Lunar. Around the bow and the part of the nerve held by the hand, are stars of Jupiter and Mars. In the face, the nebulous cluster, that is, the double nebula, has the same effect as Mars and the Moon, and belongs to the 3rd degree, 40 minutes, 12 seconds of Capricorn, with a northern latitude of 0 degrees, 45 minutes, 1 second. Those in the band and back follow the nature of Jupiter and a little of Venus. The feet are of Jupiter and Mars. The Terebellum, that is, the four-sided old woman, is like Jupiter, and gently like Mercury. The horns of Capricorn are of the nature of Saturn and a little of Venus. The stars of the mouth are Saturnian, and at the same time partake somewhat of the nature of Mercury. The feet and belly are Martian and a little of Venus. Finally, the tail follows the nature of Saturn and Jupiter. The stars in the shoulders of Aquarius, likewise in the garment and left hand, refer to the nature of Saturn and Mercury. The thighs imitate more Saturn than Jupiter. Those in the flowing water are Saturnian and partly Jovian. The last asterism of the zodiac is formed by two Fish, one southern, the other northern, which are connected by a thread, which some call a line, others a small line, others a ligament. The stars in the head of the southern Fish are of the nature of Jupiter and somewhat of Mercury. Those in the body are Jovian and Mercurial. In the tail and southern thread, they refer to Saturn and a little of Mars. Those in the body and spine of the northern Fish are Martian and somewhat also Venerean. Those in the northern thread imitate the nature of Saturn and Jupiter. Finally, the bright star in the knot has the nature of Saturn and somewhat of Mercury. The asterisms of the remaining signs outside the zodiac follow. And first, the 21st northern figure. The Little Bear or Cynosura, which the Phoenicians observe at sea, because it is closer to the pole and moves less. The star at the end of its tail, because it is closest to the axis, they call the pole: yet it is almost four parts distant from the true vertex of the world. The bright stars of this bear have the quality of Saturn, and somewhat of Venus. The Great Bear or Helice, which the Greeks, because it has more prominent stars, observe at sea. For this reason, in Homer, the Achaeans are called 'helico-eyed'. The stars of this bear resemble Jupiter, and under its tail, the Coma Berenices has the temperament of Jupiter and Venus. Therefore, these stars are as if very moist hair, and portend suffocations from waters or phlegm. Both bears are interwoven with seven principal stars, which they call the Septentriones. The common people and poets call them wagons from the shape of horses and wheels. Homer in Iliad 6: 'the bear, which they also call by the name of wagon'. The bright stars of Draco, which winds around the two bears like a river with a sinuous bend, are of the nature of Saturn, Mars, and Jupiter. Cepheus, the husband of Cassiopeia, which constellation the Babylonians called Ficares, and some among us called 'inflamed' and 'lord of flame', contains stars of Jupiter and Saturn. Bootes, that is, the herdsman, is a constellation that follows Helice or the Great Bear, also called Arctophylax and guardian of the Erimanthian bear. Ovid in Fasti 3: 'Whether it is Arctophylax, or that lazy Bootes.' The stars of the Bear set with difficulty, the Southern stars rise with difficulty, therefore Bootes is often called slow and lazy by the poets because of his long stay. Thus Homer in the Odyssey called him 'the setting Bootes'. Lucan in Pharsalia book 3 called him swift, but that must be referred to the position of the southern parts of the earth, where Bootes does not remain in the upper hemisphere for as long as in northern places: his words are these: 'Then Roman fury moved the distant Oretas and German leaders, whose sky, deflected to the south, does not see the entire bear submerged, yet it shines, and swift Bootes is there for a short night.' The stars of Bootes have the qualities of Saturn and Mercury, except that the reddish and shining star of the first magnitude located between Bootes' feet, which the Chaldeans call Azemer Aramer, and our people Arcturus, that is, the tail of the bear, retains the qualities of Mars and Jupiter. It falls in the 18th degree, 40 minutes of Libra, with a northern latitude of 31 degrees. The Northern Crown of Ariadne, which they say Bacchus placed among the stars, whence it is also called Gnosia: the Chaldeans call it Malfelcare. It contains stars that act like Venus, Mercury, and Jupiter. One, brighter than the others, is called by some Hercules, by others Theseus, by others Thamyris: it is the one.
