Prima cautela: Luna combuſta non poteſt eſſe ilech, imò hocfi, &c, nullus planeta combuſtus poteſt efſſe vitæ ſignificator.De arcubus autem emerſionum & occultationum, intra quos ſtellæ ra dijs Solis oppreſſa comburuntur, fuprà abunde dictum eſt. Aſpicies 4. Secunda cautela, que quomodo intelligenda fit, patere poautem by teſt ex ijs quæ ſuprà de diſtributione 12. domiciliorum ex ſenlech, &c. tentia Ioannis de Regiomonte & aliorum diximus. Cumques Tertia cautela, quę etiam cum doctrina Ptolemai conſen- #quaue- tit, ſed apud Ptolemeum vna domus protenditur ſpacio 30. par ris do. &c. rium æquino&ialis circuli, quarum quinque ante cuſpidem do mus, & vigintiquinque mox ſequentes virtutem unius domus obtinent. At autor videtur loqui de partibus zodiaci. 6 quarta cautela, que à Ptolemei do&rina diſſentit.ἀφίας ſeu Et omnis Ilegium nulli decerni poteſt, niſi concurrat quoq; Alcocodes, locus. hoc eft, planeta qui in loco Ilech dominatur, eundemq; aliquo aſpectu intuetur. Quicunque enim planeta maiorem prærogatiuam in loco Ilech habuerit, eundemq; locum reſpexerit, eum vocant Alcocodes. Quòd fi quando contingat plures ſimul atteſtari, potior in vtroque aſpectu ſcilicet & dignitate præferen dus eft:qui enim habuerit plures dignitates, & fuerit ei propin quior gradibus, is erit Alcocodes. Denique ſi aut) Apheta fuerit, fitq; in domicilio proprio aut exaltatiõe, tunc ſimul erit lech & Alcocodes, quando videlicet non habuerit aſpicienté aliqué de dominis dignitatum eſſentialium. Sumuntaût Arabes ex Alcocodeo coniecturas de longæuitate vel breuitate vitæ geniti. Nam Alcocodes repertus in angulo annos fuos maiores, in ſuccedenti, annos medios, in cadenti, annos minores argiri dicitur. Nulla huius rei exempla adferam, proptereà per ſe ſatis clara fit, & quòd hæ coniecture valde infirme fint, & à Ptolemzi do&rina diſſentiant, neq; vilam phyſicam rationem præ ſe ferre videantur. Sanè ſi dominus loci potens locum ipfum, & præcipuè bono aſpectu vident, Aphetam robuftiorem reddit, & ob id vitam nato robuſtiorem & longiorem præſtat, teſte Ptolemzo, qui in locis Apheticis & interemptorijs, quibus ſuffragijs ca fulcita ſunt, diligenter penſitandu eſſe docuit. Planeta : Digitized by Google ; : 1 391 QVARTA. Planeta ergo ſigni Aphetici amicus, ſi aphetæ radio ſuo affulgeat, quamuis roboris multum ipſi adijciat, ne ex leui cauſa ſuc cumbat,vtq; Aneratas fibi occurrentes facilius ſuperet: tamen certum numerum annorum vitæ, qui maiore induſtria & labore perquirendus eſt, ſine craſsiſsimis erroribus vix indica re poterit. 7 Victorem ſeu dominum genituræ Arabes vocant Almuten Almutes natiuitatis, Gręci κύριομ γενέσεως : eſtq; Planeta qui plures po- eſt qui prei tiorefq; prærogatiuas obtinet in quinque locis Aphetaru, nem- eſt natiui, pè loco Solis, loco Lunæ, loco Cardinis exorientis, loco fortis tati &, fortunarum, loco & vel & luminarium proximè natiuitatem, præcedentis. Quicunque ergo ex planetis in dictis locis maiorem potioremque ſuffragiorum copiam obtinuerit, is dominium genitura ſortietur. Per ſuffragia autem hic intelligimus dominium domus, altitudinis, trigoni & finium. Denique con iunctionem & qualemcunque aſpectum alium, imò etiam intuentia imperantiaque. Iulius Firmicus lib.4.cap. 12. eum eſſe dominum genituræ putat, cuius ſignum poſt natum hominem ( primò ingreſſura eſt. Veluti ſi quis prima natali hora Lunam in Ariete habuerit, quia Lunæ poſt ad ४ ſecundo loco facit tranfitum, & dominium geniturz adipiſcetur, eò quòd fit ſignum &, & fic in cæteris ſignis. In hoc modo obſeruandum eſt, quòd neque neque (in aliqua genitura domini effician tur. Quare ſi fuerit in IT inuenta co die quo naſcitur homo, tunc neque in 55, hoc eft, in domo ſua, neque in domo Solis genituræ dominum ſignificabit: ſed cùm hæc ſigna tranſierit, & ad aliud fignum venerit, tunc geniturę dominum indicabit, Quia itaq; uy domus & eſt, is dominus genituræ efficietur. Si cut autem pleraque alia Firmici, ita hoc quoque preceptum diſſentit à vera do&rina Ptolemei, etiamfi aliquam ſimilitudinem habere videatur cum Ptolemai regula, qua locum planete aut radium, ſuper qué primò ſignificator motu diurno cecide, rit, obſeruare precipit, vt infra videbimus.Sed hoc ratio alia eſt. De profectione ſignorum. : Et ex hoc fignum profectionis in annis natiuitatum atque Digitized by Google. DIFFERENTIA atque mundi. 2. Nam in annis natiuitatum ſcientia eſt,vt aſpicias quot anni tranſierunt nato ex annis ſolaribus perfectis, aſpiciesque omni anno ſignum vnum, I incipies ab aſcendente nati per ſucceſsionem ſignorum, quousque finitus fuerit numerus, fignum quod ſuccedit eſt ſignum profectionis ab aſcendente, ſignum videlicet anni intratis, quem non miſiſti in numero, hoc erit in ta li gradu ſicut fuit in aſcendente natiuitatis: & dominus eiufdem dicitur alcochoden:cuius interpretatio eſt domi nus anni. Similiter numerabis à ſigno Solis vnicuiq; anno ſignum vnum, & exibit per hoc ſignum profectionis à Sole.Similiter à ſigno Lunæ & à medio cæli, à par te fortune in fimilitudinem gradus radicis, id eſt, puncti natiuitatis. Cuius rei éxemplar eſt, quòd quidam natus eſt oriente Capricorno, ſcilicet XVII.gradu eius: 15 Sol erat in piſcibus XV. gradu: & medium cæli Scorpionis octo grad. Luna in Libra XV.grad.& pars fortuna in Leone XVII. grad. & iam tranſierunt nato tres an. ni perfecti, peruenietque quartus annus ab aſcédente in Ariete, qui eſt quartus à Capricorno vſque ad XVII. gradum eiufdem Arietis, & erit Mars alcochoden: peruenit locus Solis ad Geminos vſque ad XV.gra.eius dem ſigni:& peruenit Luna ad Capricornum ad XV. grad. profectus à medio cæli ad octo grad. Aquarij, profectus à parte fortune peruenit vſque ad XVII. gradum Scorpý. 3 Cumque peruenerit annus ad gradun profectionis, vt ad XVII.gradum
translation: They also say that the Moon opposing the Ascendant kills, especially if it has dignity in the eighth house. This is Ptolemy's opinion on this matter. It remains for us to briefly depict, as if in a table, which authors teach us to choose the significators of life from the opinions of others. In diurnal nativities, look within 5 degrees that have risen above the horizon at the end of the twelfth house. First, in the tenth, which, if it is in the eleventh, if it is in the seventh, eighth, ninth. Ascendant, second, third, seventh. Second, look within 5 degrees that have risen above the horizon, which, if it is in the tenth, in the eleventh, in the fourth, fifth, in any masculine sign, the Sun will be there. If the Moon is in any feminine sign only, within 5 degrees preceding the cusp of the seventh. In nocturnal nativities, look within 5 degrees that have risen above the horizon. First, in the tenth, which, if it is in the eleventh, if it is in the seventh, in the eighth, in the ninth. In its masculine sign, the Moon will be there, only if it is masculine. Second, within 5 degrees before the cusp of the seventh house, below the earth, in any sign, the Sun, which, if it is in the fifth, will be there. In the Ascendant, in a masculine sign, in the second. If neither luminary is found suitable for the Hyleg, and the degree of conjunction precedes the nativity and is in an angle or succedent house. Opposition of the opposition: If these places are not found suitable enough for the Hyleg, then the Part of Fortune will moderate life, if it is found in angles or succedent houses; if not, the degree of the Horoscopus will prolong life. But the types of signs are not to be considered here, but only in the luminaries. According to this reasoning, the Sun has six Aphetic places above the earth, namely, 5 degrees above the horizon, the tenth, eleventh, seventh, eighth, and ninth. Below the earth, five: namely, five degrees before the seventh, the fourth, fifth, the Horoscopus, and the second house. The Moon, however, has five Aphetic places above the earth: namely, five degrees preceding the Horoscopus, the tenth, eleventh, seventh, and eighth. Below the earth, six: namely, the Ascendant, the second house, the third, fourth, fifth, and five degrees preceding the cusp of the west. First caution: A combust Moon cannot be a Hyleg; indeed, no combust planet can be a significator of life. Concerning the arcs of emergence and occultation, within which stars oppressed by the Sun's rays are combust, enough has been said above. You will also look at the Hyleg, etc. Second caution, how this is to be understood can be seen from what we have said above concerning the distribution of the 12 houses according to the opinion of John of Regiomontanus and others. Third caution, which also agrees with Ptolemy's doctrine, but in Ptolemy, one house extends over a space of 30 parts of the equinoctial circle, of which five before the cusp of the house and twenty-five immediately following obtain the virtue of one house. But the author seems to be speaking of parts of the zodiac. Fourth caution, which dissents from Ptolemy's doctrine. No Hyleg can be assigned unless the Alcocoden also concurs, that is, the planet that rules in the place of the Hyleg and aspects it with some aspect. For whatever planet has the greater prerogative in the place of the Hyleg and aspects that same place, they call it the Alcocoden. If it happens that several attest at the same time, the one superior in both aspect and dignity is to be preferred: for he who has more dignities and is closer to it in degrees will be the Alcocoden. Finally, if the Apheta is in its own domicile or exaltation, then it will be both Hyleg and Alcocoden, when, that is, it has no aspecting planet from the lords of essential dignities. The Arabs derive conjectures about the longevity or brevity of life from the Alcocoden. For the Alcocoden found in an angle is said to signify its greater years, in a succedent house, its middle years, in a cadent house, its lesser years. I will not adduce any examples of this, because it is clear enough by itself, and because these conjectures are very weak and dissent from Ptolemy's doctrine, nor do they seem to present any physical reason. Indeed, if the lord of the place powerfully aspects the place itself, and especially with a good aspect, it renders the Apheta stronger, and thereby provides a stronger and longer life to the native, as Ptolemy testifies, who taught that in Aphetic and killing places, with what suffrages they are supported, must be diligently weighed. Therefore, a planet friendly to the Aphetic sign, if it shines upon the Apheta with its ray, although it adds much strength to it, so that it does not succumb for a slight cause, and that it may more easily overcome the Aneratas that oppose it: nevertheless, it will hardly be able to indicate a certain number of years of life, which must be sought with greater industry and labor, without the grossest errors. The Arabs call the Victor or Lord of the nativity the Almuten of the nativity, the Greeks κύριος γενέσεως: it is the planet that obtains more and better prerogatives in the five Aphetic places, namely, the place of the Sun, the place of the Moon, the place of the eastern angle, the place of the Part of Fortune, and the place of the luminaries closest to the nativity. Therefore, whichever of the planets obtains the greater and more powerful abundance of suffrages in the said places, that one will obtain the lordship of the nativity. By suffrages here we understand the rulership of the house, exaltation, trigon, and terms. Finally, conjunction and any other aspect, indeed, even ruling and commanding. Julius Firmicus, in Book 4, Chapter 12, believes that the lord of the nativity is the one whose sign the Moon will first enter after the person is born. For example, if someone had the Moon in Aries at the first hour of birth, because the Moon makes its transit to Taurus in the second place, it will obtain the lordship of the nativity, because it is the sign of Taurus, and so in the other signs. In this way, it must be observed that neither the Sun nor the Moon can be made lords in any nativity. Therefore, if the Moon is found in Taurus on the day a person is born, then neither in Taurus, that is, in its own house, nor in the house of the Sun, will it signify the lord of the nativity: but when it has passed these signs and come to another sign, then it will indicate the lord of the nativity. Because Taurus is the house of Venus, Venus will be made the lord of the nativity. However, just as many other precepts of Firmicus, so also this one dissents from the true doctrine of Ptolemy, even if it seems to have some similarity with Ptolemy's rule, by which he teaches to observe the place of the planet or the ray upon which the significator first fell by diurnal motion, as we will see below. But this is another reason. Concerning the profection of signs. And from this, the sign of profection in the years of nativities and of the world. For in the years of nativities, the knowledge is to look at how many years have passed for the native from the perfect solar years, and you will look at one sign each year, starting from the Ascendant of the native by the succession of signs, until the number is finished. The sign that succeeds is the sign of profection from the Ascendant, namely, the sign of the entered year, which you have not included in the number. This will be in the same degree as it was in the Ascendant of the nativity: and its lord is called the Alcochoden, whose interpretation is the lord of the year. Similarly, you will count from the sign of the Sun one sign for each year, and from this will come the sign of profection from the Sun. Similarly, from the sign of the Moon and from the Midheaven, from the Part of Fortune in the likeness of the degree of the radix, that is, the point of nativity. An example of this is that someone was born with Capricorn rising, namely, in its 17th degree: the Sun was in Pisces in the 15th degree: and the Midheaven was in Scorpio in the 8th degree. The Moon was in Libra in the 15th degree, and the Part of Fortune in Leo in the 17th degree. And now three perfect years have passed for the native, and the fourth year will arrive from the Ascendant in Aries, which is the fourth from Capricorn up to the 17th degree of that same Aries, and Mars will be the Alcochoden: the place of the Sun arrives at Gemini up to the 15th degree of that same sign: and the Moon arrives at Capricorn at the 15th degree. The profection from the Midheaven arrives at the 8th degree of Aquarius, the profection from the Part of Fortune arrives at the 17th degree of Scorpio. And when the year arrives at the degree of profection, as at the 17th degree.

author: Valentin Naibod
title: Profection of the World's Years
text: Arietis in hoc exemplo: & fuerit inter Digitized by Google QUARTA 393 inter ipfum & fimilegradū fuccedētis figni, ad que deber peruenire annus ſeques, in eadě domo planeta vel radije. ius:& volueris fcire quado puenerit profectio ad ipſum planetā vel ad radios eius:afpicies qd fit inter gradu ad que pueniet annus, & planeta velradios ex gradibus g minutis: multiplicabis illud p XII. & fexta illius nu meri, que multiplicas velin XII. gra. VI. vnius grad. quot prouenerint, erût dies ex anno illo in quo fueris, in quibus pueniet pfectio à gradu in quo incipit, fq; ad gradu ad que numeraſti, poft fiet pfectio ipfius planete. 4 Profectio aute ex annis mundi dixit Alchindus eft, quod fuerit inter annu cõiŭctionis a ſignificauit Sarrace norūfectă, Finter anni Albegerat in quo cæpit primu p dicare Mahometus, q fuit primus annoru Arabū, LII. anni Sotares & LVII. dies: & fuit afcendēs anni illius cõiunctiois pdicta ſectæ fignú Geminoru: puenit pro fectio eiufde anni ad Virgine Ginter ipfum primú anno ru Arabu, primú annorú Iezdygird regis Perfaru fwe rut 3624.diesſcilicet perfecti. Cum ergo volueris habere notitia huius rei:accipe annos Iezdygird, verte eos in dies, ficut ia expoſitu eft in azig, ideft, in libro curfuŭ pla netaru:adde defup dies qui funt inter primú annoru Arabū lezdygird:todinide hoc p CCC.LXV.dies & quarta parte diei: quot diuifiões exierint, tot erut an ni Solares:& quod remäferit ex mēſibus diebus, erit ex anno imperfecto:quod cũ ita collectű fuerit ex annis, ipfi funt anni Solares ab initio annorű Arabu, proijce er go omni anno figuŭ vnú es incipe à Virgine, ad quod d cung Digitized by Google 394 DIFFERENTIA cúq; fignú te pduxerit numerus, ipfum erit fignú ad quod puenit annus mudi ab aſceſione cõiunctiõis pdicta fecta. Alijs autě extra alkindū viſum eft,vt adderet fup annos lezdygird pfectosLXI.annos, II.mēſes, XII. dies XVI.horas, ex annis Perfidi:qui funt fine fractione, id eft,fine quarta diei, Gextenderent hos annos in dies, ac verterent dies in annosSolares,ficut prediximus, inciperet proijcere ab initio Libra. Quòd fi volueris pro fectionem à figno coniüctionis ſecta, fit proiectio à Scor pione:fi verò volueris profectionem ab afcedente regni, minue ex annis Iezdygird annos illos CCCXVII.perfio dos, & verte eos in annos Solares ficut pradiximus: & incipe projcere à Virgine. Rurfus fi volueris profectio. nem ab afcenfione profectionis mutationis cõiunctionis à triplicitate aquatica ad triplicitatem igneam, minue ex annis Iezdygird perfectis CLXXVI. annos, & ver te te illos illos qui qui reman remanent in annos Solares, incipe proj cere à Leone, & quò peruenerit numerus in eode ſigno, erit profectus vniufcuiusque initij eorum quæ diximus. Et ex hoc I Loca Aphetica tempore rerum exortus in certis partibus fignú pro- figniferi deprehenſa non manent fixa, ſed annuis, menſtruis difectio.&c. urniſque reuolutionibus interuallo 30. graduű zodiaci in con- ſequentia figna promouentur. Talem horum locorum εἰς τὰ ἑπόμνα progreſsionem, vulgò profectionem nominant,cuius duas ſpecies hoc in loco autor ponit. Vna eſt profectio natiuitatum, altera annorum mundi. Nam in 2 Acannua quidem quinq; locorum Apheticorum in genitu annis nati ris hominum profectio eſt, in qua generaliter vnicuique anno uitatu &c. Solari ſpacium vnius figni, id eft, 30.partes deputantur. Planetam verò qui huiufmodi ſigno dominatur, dominum anni ſeu Chronocratorem annuum vocant. Quòdfi præditz 30.partes duorum Digitized by Google QVARTA. I 395 duorum diftin&orum fignorum terminis concludantur, ficut plærunque accidit, tunc quoque dominium annuum diuerfis planetis per partes temporis diſtribuitur. Verbi gratia:Si in genitura alicuius 12.part. in exorientem cardinem incidat, primus annus habebit ſpacium ſigniferi, quod à parte 12. ad par tem 12.8 protenditur. Et ita primis 7. menfibus & 9. diebus præerunt & pro 18.partibus.Reſiduumanni,hoceft, men ſes 4.dies 26. gubernabunt) & &, qui ijdem planetę primam partem ſequentis anni diſponent, ita vt dominium vltima par tis eiufdem ſequentis anni deuoluatur ad & Geminorum do. minum. In fine tertij anni pertinget profectio ad par. 12.55, eritque Chronocrator eius cum 4:& fic confequenter iuxta fignorum ſuccedentium ordinem annuos temporum arbitros ordinabimus, vſque ad annum duodecimum, quo completo, anno tertiodecimo profectio ad ἐποχὴμ ſeu ad locum radicis,id eſt, ad gradum cardinis exorientis, dominumque & o rurfum reuertetur, eafdemque ad vices exibit:atque hoc modo, vt di&um eft, quilibet locus Apheticus, loca hæc cæli ad finem vi tzorbiculariter luftrans, duodecimo quoque anno vnam periodum complebit. Quòd fi ergo pluribus annis nato exactis ſcire cupias, quem locum profectio horoscopi inambulet, partiaris annos nati completos per 12.& numerum qui remanet extende ab horoscopo co modo, vt monuimus. Viuatiam,exepli gratia, natus, cuius horoſcopus fuit par. 14. annum labentem 40. Quare habebit annos completos 39.qui per 12.fecti relinquunt 3.annos completos, quorum primum Leoni, fecun dum my, tertium tribuamus, anno ætatis nati labenti, hoc eſt, anno ætatis nati 40. horoſcopum ad par. 14. Scorpionis proceſsiſſe intelligemus, ac o ſigni illius dominum, chronocratorem atque anni huiufmodi principalem dominum conſtituemus, donec eadem progreſsio in feratur, quod fiet poſt reuo lutionis dies 194. ferè: ſiquidem profectio annua fingulis die bus ſcrupula prima 4. & præterea ſecundaria ſcrupula 56.peragrat, tum enim 4. dominus Sagittarij, Marti in imperio annuo fuccedet. Hæc autem quæ di&a funt de profectione horofcopi, ex cuius applicatione ad benigniores vel prauiores planetarum radiationes bona vel mala accidentia in corpore nati illo anno d 2 obuen- ( Digitized by Google 396 DIFFEREΝΤΙΑ obuentura przuidentur, pari penitus ratione &mode de grada culminante pro rmagifterio, de gradu pro dignitate & hono re, deque reliquis ilegiorum partibus pro accidentibus alijs in telligenda funt. Sed hæc omnia in genituris, quas accuratius perſpicere volumus, præſtabit diſtribuere per tabulam, quam in hunc modum conficies. In prima ſerie ponanturanni autis nati à ad 12. In fecunda à 13. ad 24. In tertia à 25.in 36.in quaraab 37.ad 48.& fic deinceps vfque ad annos vitæ nati vltimos. Pofteà in vertice annorum ætatis atque ſerie prima ſcribantur vertentes anni Chriſti, qui initium genituræ continent, quibus mox addantur 12.atque quod hinc naſcitur, ponatur in faftigio fecundæ ſeriei, ſeu fupra annum ætatis 13.& fic deinceps 12.annos addendo pergendum eft, donec ad ſeriem annorum ætatis nati vltimam deueniatur:quo facto è regione anni 1.ætatis ponenda eft in figno & gradu epocha horoscopi, deinde epocha Luna:tertid epocha: quartò epocha M.C. quintò & vltimè epocha partis fortunarum. Hæc verticalia figna ſi ſecundum naturalem ſeriem ad pedem tabulæ vſque deducantur, tabula profe&ionum annuarum abſoluetur, ficut videre eſt in genituraaffumpta, cuius quinquelocorum Apheticorum ἐποχάς & profectiones exempli gratia tibi hic πινακικός ſubiecimus. translation: In this example of Aries: and if it were between the 393rd degree between itself and a similar degree of the succeeding sign, to which the following year ought to arrive, in the same house a planet or its rays: and you wish to know when the profection will reach that planet or its rays: you will look at what is between the degree to which the year will arrive, and the planet or its rays, in degrees and minutes: you will multiply that by 12. And the sixth part of that number, which you multiply, or in 12 degrees, 6 minutes of one degree, whatever results, will be the days of that year in which you are, in which the profection will arrive from the degree in which it begins, up to the degree to which you have counted, after which the profection of that planet will occur. Alchindus said that the profection from the years of the world is that which was between the year of the conjunction that signified the Saracen sect, and the year of Albegerat in which Muhammad first began to preach, which was the first of the Arab years, 52 years and 57 days: and the ascendant of that year of the conjunction of the aforesaid sect was the sign of Gemini: the profection of that same year arrived at Virgo, and between the first of the Arab years and the first of the years of King Iezdygird of the Persians, there were 3624 perfect days. Therefore, when you wish to have knowledge of this matter: take the years of Iezdygird, convert them into days, as has already been explained in the Azig, that is, in the book of the courses of the planets: add the days that are between the first of the Arab years and Iezdygird: then divide this by 365 days and a quarter of a day: as many divisions as come out, so many will be the Solar years: and what remains of months and days will be from the imperfect year: when this has been collected from the years, these are the Solar years from the beginning of the Arab years, therefore cast out one sign for each year and begin from Virgo, to whatever sign the number leads you, that will be the sign to which the year of the world arrives from the ascendant of the conjunction of the aforesaid sect. Others, however, outside of Alchindus, thought it right to add to the perfect years of Iezdygird 61 years, 2 months, 12 days, 16 hours, from the Persian years: which are without fraction, that is, without a quarter of a day, and they would extend these years into days, and convert the days into Solar years, as we have said, and begin to cast out from the beginning of Libra. But if you wish the profection from the sign of the conjunction of the sect, let the profection be from Scorpio: but if you wish the profection from the ascendant of the kingdom, subtract from the years of Iezdygird those 317 perfect years, and convert them into Solar years as we have said: and begin to cast out from Virgo. Again, if you wish the profection from the ascendant of the profection of the change of conjunction from the watery triplicity to the fiery triplicity, subtract from the perfect years of Iezdygird 176 years, and convert those that remain into Solar years, begin to cast out from Leo, and to whatever sign the number arrives, that will be the profection of each beginning of those things we have mentioned. And from this, the Aphetic places at the time of the origin of things, found in certain parts of the zodiac, do not remain fixed, but are moved annually, monthly, and daily by revolutions of 30 degrees of the zodiac into succeeding signs. The author here places two kinds of such progression of these places, commonly called profection. One is the profection of nativities, the other of the years of the world. For the annual profection of the five Aphetic places in the nativity of men is that in which generally for each Solar year, the space of one sign, that is, 30 parts, is allotted. The planet, however, which rules such a sign, they call the lord of the year or the annual Chronocrator. But if the aforesaid 30 parts are concluded by the boundaries of two distinct signs, as often happens, then also the annual dominion is distributed to different planets by parts of time. For example: If in someone's nativity 12 parts fall into the eastern angle, the first year will have the space of the zodiac which extends from the 12th part to the 12th part. And so for the first 7 months and 9 days, they will preside for 18 parts. The remainder of the year, that is, 4 months and 26 days, will be governed by and, which same planets will dispose of the first part of the following year, so that the dominion of the last part of the same following year devolves to the lord of Gemini. At the end of the third year, the profection will reach part 12, and its Chronocrator will be with 4: and so consequently according to the order of the succeeding signs, we will arrange the annual arbiters of times, up to the twelfth year, which being completed, in the thirteenth year the profection will return to the epoch or to the radical place, that is, to the degree of the eastern angle, and the lord and will return again, and will proceed in the same way: and in this manner, as has been said, each Aphetic place, traversing these places of the sky orbicularly to the end of life, will complete one period every twelfth year. Therefore, if you wish to know, after many years of a native's life have passed, what place the profection of the horoscope ambulates, divide the completed years of the native by 12, and extend the remaining number from the horoscope in the manner we have advised. For example, let a native live, whose horoscope was part 14, in the 40th year. Therefore, he will have 39 completed years, which divided by 12 leave 3 completed years, the first of which we assign to Leo, the second to, the third to, in the current year of the native's age, that is, in the 40th year of the native's age, we will understand the horoscope to have proceeded to part 14 of Scorpio, and we will constitute the lord of that sign, the chronocrator and the principal lord of such a year, until the same progression is introduced, which will happen after about 194 days of revolution: since the annual profection traverses 4 first scruples and besides 56 secondary scruples every day, then 4, the lord of Sagittarius, will succeed Mars in annual dominion. But these things which have been said about the profection of the horoscope, from whose application to the more benevolent or more malevolent radiations of the planets good or bad accidents in the native's body in that year are foreseen, are to be understood in exactly the same way and manner concerning the culminating degree for mastery, concerning the degree for dignity and honor, and concerning the remaining parts of the lots for other accidents. But all these things in nativities, which we wish to examine more accurately, it will be better to distribute by a table, which you will construct in this way. In the first series, place the years of the native from 1 to 12. In the second from 13 to 24. In the third from 25 to 36. In the fourth from 37 to 48. And so on up to the last years of the native's life. Afterwards, at the top of the years of age and in the first series, write the current years of Christ, which contain the beginning of the nativity, to which immediately add 12, and what arises from this, place at the top of the second series, or above the 13th year of age, and so on, adding 12 years, until the last series of the native's years of age is reached: this done, opposite the 1st year of age, the epoch of the horoscope is to be placed in the sign and degree, then the epoch of the Moon: third the epoch: fourth the epoch of the MC: fifth and last the epoch of the part of fortune. If these vertical signs are led down to the foot of the table according to their natural series, the table of annual profections will be completed, as can be seen in the assumed nativity, whose epochs and profections of the five Aphetic places we have here presented to you tabularly as an example. author: Valentin Naibod
title: Direction of Significators in Quadrants
text: ==Start of OCR for page 1== 408 BIFFERENTIA li direfli plus fignificatore regionis, mintes equationem de fignificatore circuli directi: & quod ex eo exierit,erit gradus directionis. 4 Quòd fi fuerit fignificator in aliqua quarta, locus ad quem diriges eum,in alia quarta, diriges fignificatorem ad angulum qui ei fuccedit:post hoc diriges ab angulo ad locum, quemadmodum oftendi tibi directionem ab angulo, & iunges vtraſque directiones. 5 Quòd cum ſciueris fignificatorem, volueris fcire quo perueniet directio de circulo ſignoru in aliquo annorum: fuerita significator in afcendente: addes numerum annorum fuper afcenfiones gradus afcendentis:& facies eum arcum in afcenfione regionis:et quo exiuerit arcus grad. de circulo fignorum, ibi pero ueniet directio in eodem anno. Si verò fuerit fignificator in VII. addes numerum annorum fuper afcenfiones in VII. addes numerum an nadir gradus fignificatoris : & arcuabis eum etiam in afcenfionibus regionis: & quod exierit de circulo fignorum, ad eius nadir perueniet directio eodem anno. Quòd fi fuerit fignificator in X.vel IIII.addes fuper afcenfior nes gradus fignificatoris in circulo directo numerum an norum: & arcuabis eum in afcefionibus circuli directi, &quod exierit de circulo ſignorum, ibidem perueniet in eodem anno directio. Si verò fuerit fignificator extra hac quatuor loca que funt anguli:fcias horas longitudi nis ab angulo, ficut prædixi: quòd fi fuerit fignificator in medietate orientali: addes fuper aſcenſiones grad.finificatoris per circulum directum numerum annorum quos volueris: & arcuabis koc in afcenfionibus circuli directi, Digitized by Google ==Start of OCR for page 2== QUARTA. 409: directi:& quod exierit, erit ſignificator circuli directi, ferua eum.Poft hoc addes numerum annorum etiam fue per afcenfiones grad.fignificatoris in regione: arcua bis hoc in aſcenſione regionis:& quod fuerit, erit ſignifi cator regionis. Poft hoc accipies fextam partem refidui inter fignificatorem circuli directi, ſignificatorem regionis: & multiplicabis eam in horas longitudinis ab angulo: quod fuerit, erit equatio. Quòd fifuerit figni ficator circuli directi minor ſignificatore regionis: ada des aquationem ſuper ſignificatorem circuli directi: fi fuerit plus, minues eam de eo:& quod remanferit, ibi perueniet directio ex circulo fignorum eodem anno. Quòd fi fuerit fignificator in medietate occidentali,fa. cies eum nadir grad. fignificatoris,ficut fecifti cum gra du fignificatoris in opere quod eſt ante eum:& quod fue vit, ibidem perueniet directio ex circulo fignorum in eo. dem anno. 6 Et locus ad quem perueniet directio, dici tur locus diuifionis: dominus termini illius loci,dici tur diuifor:quòd fi fuerit in gradu diuiſionis planeta a lius aut radij eius aut propè locum ante eum:dicitur hic planeta aut dominus radiorum,particeps diuiforis in dia uifione. 7 7 Significatores quoq; qui diriguntur, funt quinq; loca, id eft, gra.afcendentis, & gra. Solis gra. Luna, grad. Aphetæ quoque partis fortune, ac gradus medij cæli. Gradus climacto nanque afcendentis dirigitur ad daccidentia accidentia dignofcen. dignofcen rica. da, que accidunt in corpore:& grad. Solis ad dignitatis exaltationis caufas: honoris quoque atque regni : f : Luna Digitized by Google ==Start of OCR for page 3== 410 DIFFERENTIA Luna propter effe anime & corporis atque coniugy, pars fortune propter acquifitiones & profectus exigui tatem Glargitatem, & medium cæli propter magifteri. um & ceteras difpofitiones particulares & effe eorum. 8 Si autem fuerit afcendens revolutionis alicutus ane norum mundi vel natiuitatum, diriguntur ſignificato res eius fecundum hanc directionem,vnicuique fcilicet gradui diem vnum. Quibufdam videtur vt dirigantur fe gnificatores anni hoc modo, omnibus fcilicet LIX.mi. nutis VIII.fecundis vnum diem:nam directio regum principum fic dirigitur eis à gradumedy cæliper a ſceſiones circuli directi, omnibus LIX.minutis, er ofte fecundis vnum diem, donec perueniat ad bonos vel malos planetas. Et dirigitur effe regum in incolumitate, aut infirmitate ex gradu afcendentis ad banos vel malos planetas omnibus, LIX.minutis, & octo fecundis diem vnum per aſcenſiones regionis. Et directio in revolutio ne annorum mundi & prænoſcendum eſſe vulgi velru fticorum à gradu afcendentis : fimiliter & ad prenoſcen dum effe regis à gradu medij cæli, & in revolutione annorum natiuitatum ad prenoſcendum efſſe nati à gradu afcendentis revolutionis, omnibus LIX.minutis Gocto fecundis per aſcenſiones regionis diem vnum. De dire&ionibus ſeu deductionibus fignificatorum. Et ex hoc Dirigere Latinis id quod Gręcis ευθύνομ,ἢ ἰθύνου το σκάλιους ſequitur hoceft:Obliquum emendare & rectum facere ſignificat: & diata.&c. setum iter eſt, quod in ambages non deflectit, fed dire&è,& vt Græ / Digitized by Google ==Start of OCR for page 4== " QUARTA 411 Graci loquuntur, επ' ευθείας progreditur. Hinc credo rece ptum effe, vt zodiaci obliquarum & inæqualium afcenfionum reductio ad regularé æqualemque menſură dicatur direrectio. Quonia enim tam in recta quàm in obliqua ſphæra, partes Ecli pticę inæqualiter peroriuntur & occidűt, fic vt alibi plus, alibi minus téporis aſcendendo deſcendédoq; abſumat, idcirco hæc anomalia ſeu inequalis inceſſus eclipticę, modò obliquè, modò rectè incedentis emendandus, atque ad certű regularemq; motum æquinoctialis, fingulis horis in omni ſphæræ situ præciſe part. 15. attollentis deprimentiſque conferendus eſt, vt euentuum tempora progreſsione partium cæli emenſa, certò queant cognofci.Nam quando ſingulę partes, fingulos annos in hac doctrina denunciare dicuntur, id non de Eclipticæ, vt Albumafar putabat, fed de partibus æquino&ialis intelligendum eſt. Verbi gratia, lignum Leonis antequàm totum Coloniæ afcendat, horas 2. fcrup.49 14 penè conſumit. At Aquarius vnica hora ſcrupuliſque 10 14 integer exoritur, ita vt partes Leonis, par tibus Aquarij longiorem tractum efficere nemo non intelligat.Etenim fignum Leonis aſcendendo trahit eo in loco equinoctialis partes 42.fcrup.23 14, Aquarij verò fignum faltem par tes 17.fcru.25 14. Quamobrem cùm in Ecliptica vnius quidem figni partes, longitudinem coorientium partium equinoctialis facilè vnicant, alterius verò ab eodem longius abſint(fingulienim gradus Aquarii annos non implent, Leonis verò gradus vnuſquiſque plus vno anno occupat) vtrunqueadvniformem & æqualem menſuram æquino&ialis meritò examinandúerit. Talem autem obliquæ & inæqualis progreſsionis emendationem, vulgò directionem nominant, quam vocem Pontanus tan quam ineptam ſæpius reiicit, atq; inambulationem profectio nemque dicendam eſſe precipit: Gręci wίπαμ& ἄφεσι dixerunt. Quidam wίπα generis vocem eſſe, atque cuiuflibet loci quamcunque progreſsionem fignificare volunt:ἄφεσιμ verò ſpeciem inambulationis, de qua hicagimus, qua prorogator & vitæ dominus Arabicè Ilech ditus,aliuſie ſignificator progrediés, ad obuios planetas aut eorúdé aſpectus motu equinotialis ſeu diurno deducitur, imò verò non ipſe prorogator, fed potius teſte Ptolemzo.partes interimentes, prorogatore fa immoDigitized by Google ==Start of OCR for page 5== 413 DIFFERENTIA immobiliterinEcliptica nonę ſphære fixo, & circulum poſitio mis ſuſtinente ad Aphetam deducuntur. Quotiefcunque erga prorogatore in ſua ſede, qua in geniturę cæli poſitu tenuit, comorantem, maleficarum corpora, aut radii quadrati vel oppofiti earundem inuadunt, διέρχεθ dicitur, id quod vulgò abſcin dere vocant.Nam malefici aut radii eorum minaces, quando hac via in vitę moderatorem aut horoſcopum incurrunt, vitam in cidunt, aut ſaltem diſcrimen corpori adferunt. Partes verò qua ſuo ad vitę dominum appulſu mortem aut periculum vitęadferre folent, μοίρας αναιρετικά's, id eft, partes lethales interimen teſque à loci malignitate vocari, fupra dictum eſt.Hęc ex ſenté. tia Ptolemei ita difcerni poſſunt:fed nos in expoſitione autoris docendi gratia feruabimus eius & reliquorum, qui poſtPtolemeum ſcripferunt, appellationes, quibus progreſsiones vniuerſe non vfque adeò malè, vt videtur, peculiaribus appellatio nibu
translation: If the significator is in one quadrant, and the place to which you direct it is in another quadrant, you will direct the significator to the succeeding angle. After this, you will direct from the angle to the place, just as I showed you the direction from the angle, and you will combine both directions.

translation: When you know the significator and wish to know where the direction from the circle of signs will arrive in a certain number of years: if the significator is in the ascendant: you will add the number of years to the ascensions of the degree of the ascendant: and you will make it an arc in the ascension of the region: and where the arc of degrees from the circle of signs comes out, there the direction will arrive in the same year. But if the significator is in the 7th house, you will add the number of years to the ascensions in the 7th house, you will add the number of years to the nadir of the degree of the significator: and you will arc it also in the ascensions of the region: and what comes out of the circle of signs, to its nadir the direction will arrive in the same year. But if the significator is in the 10th or 4th house, you will add to the ascensions of the degree of the significator in the direct circle the number of years: and you will arc it in the ascensions of the direct circle, and what comes out of the circle of signs, there the direction will arrive in the same year. But if the significator is outside these four places which are the angles: you will know the hours of longitude from the angle, as I said before: if the significator is in the eastern half: you will add to the ascensions of the degree of the significator through the direct circle the number of years you wish: and you will arc this in the ascensions of the direct circle, and what comes out, will be the significator of the direct circle, keep it. After this, you will also add the number of years to the ascensions of the degree of the significator in the region: you will arc this in the ascension of the region: and what it is, will be the significator of the region. After this, you will take the sixth part of the remainder between the significator of the direct circle and the significator of the region: and you will multiply it by the hours of longitude from the angle: what it is, will be the equation. But if the significator of the direct circle is less than the significator of the region: you will add the equation to the significator of the direct circle: if it is more, you will subtract it from it: and what remains, there the direction will arrive from the circle of signs in the same year. But if the significator is in the western half, you will make it the nadir of the degree of the significator, as you did with the degree of the significator in the work before it: and what it is, there the direction will arrive from the circle of signs in the same year.

author: Valentin Naibod
title: Locus Divisionis and Divisor
text: ==Start of OCR for page 1== 408 BIFFERENTIA li direfli plus fignificatore regionis, mintes equationem de fignificatore circuli directi: & quod ex eo exierit,erit gradus directionis. 4 Quòd fi fuerit fignificator in aliqua quarta, locus ad quem diriges eum,in alia quarta, diriges fignificatorem ad angulum qui ei fuccedit:post hoc diriges ab angulo ad locum, quemadmodum oftendi tibi directionem ab angulo, & iunges vtraſque directiones. 5 Quòd cum ſciueris fignificatorem, volueris fcire quo perueniet directio de circulo ſignoru in aliquo annorum: fuerita significator in afcendente: addes numerum annorum fuper afcenfiones gradus afcendentis:& facies eum arcum in afcenfione regionis:et quo exiuerit arcus grad. de circulo fignorum, ibi pero ueniet directio in eodem anno. Si verò fuerit fignificator in VII. addes numerum annorum fuper afcenfiones in VII. addes numerum an nadir gradus fignificatoris : & arcuabis eum etiam in afcenfionibus regionis: & quod exierit de circulo fignorum, ad eius nadir perueniet directio eodem anno. Quòd fi fuerit fignificator in X.vel IIII.addes fuper afcenfior nes gradus fignificatoris in circulo directo numerum an norum: & arcuabis eum in afcefionibus circuli directi, &quod exierit de circulo ſignorum, ibidem perueniet in eodem anno directio. Si verò fuerit fignificator extra hac quatuor loca que funt anguli:fcias horas longitudi nis ab angulo, ficut prædixi: quòd fi fuerit fignificator in medietate orientali: addes fuper aſcenſiones grad.finificatoris per circulum directum numerum annorum quos volueris: & arcuabis koc in afcenfionibus circuli directi, Digitized by Google ==Start of OCR for page 2== QUARTA. 409: directi:& quod exierit, erit ſignificator circuli directi, ferua eum.Poft hoc addes numerum annorum etiam fue per afcenfiones grad.fignificatoris in regione: arcua bis hoc in aſcenſione regionis:& quod fuerit, erit ſignifi cator regionis. Poft hoc accipies fextam partem refidui inter fignificatorem circuli directi, ſignificatorem regionis: & multiplicabis eam in horas longitudinis ab angulo: quod fuerit, erit equatio. Quòd fifuerit figni ficator circuli directi minor ſignificatore regionis: ada des aquationem ſuper ſignificatorem circuli directi: fi fuerit plus, minues eam de eo:& quod remanferit, ibi perueniet directio ex circulo fignorum eodem anno. Quòd fi fuerit fignificator in medietate occidentali,fa. cies eum nadir grad. fignificatoris,ficut fecifti cum gra du fignificatoris in opere quod eſt ante eum:& quod fue vit, ibidem perueniet directio ex circulo fignorum in eo. dem anno. 6 Et locus ad quem perueniet directio, dici tur locus diuifionis: dominus termini illius loci,dici tur diuifor:quòd fi fuerit in gradu diuiſionis planeta a lius aut radij eius aut propè locum ante eum:dicitur hic planeta aut dominus radiorum,particeps diuiforis in dia uifione. 7 7 Significatores quoq; qui diriguntur, funt quinq; loca, id eft, gra.afcendentis, & gra. Solis gra. Luna, grad. Aphetæ quoque partis fortune, ac gradus medij cæli. Gradus climacto nanque afcendentis dirigitur ad daccidentia accidentia dignofcen. dignofcen rica. da, que accidunt in corpore:& grad. Solis ad dignitatis exaltationis caufas: honoris quoque atque regni : f : Luna Digitized by Google ==Start of OCR for page 3== 410 DIFFERENTIA Luna propter effe anime & corporis atque coniugy, pars fortune propter acquifitiones & profectus exigui tatem Glargitatem, & medium cæli propter magifteri. um & ceteras difpofitiones particulares & effe eorum. 8 Si autem fuerit afcendens revolutionis alicutus ane norum mundi vel natiuitatum, diriguntur ſignificato res eius fecundum hanc directionem,vnicuique fcilicet gradui diem vnum. Quibufdam videtur vt dirigantur fe gnificatores anni hoc modo, omnibus fcilicet LIX.mi. nutis VIII.fecundis vnum diem:nam directio regum principum fic dirigitur eis à gradumedy cæliper a ſceſiones circuli directi, omnibus LIX.minutis, er ofte fecundis vnum diem, donec perueniat ad bonos vel malos planetas. Et dirigitur effe regum in incolumitate, aut infirmitate ex gradu afcendentis ad banos vel malos planetas omnibus, LIX.minutis, & octo fecundis diem vnum per aſcenſiones regionis. Et directio in revolutio ne annorum mundi & prænoſcendum eſſe vulgi velru fticorum à gradu afcendentis : fimiliter & ad prenoſcen dum effe regis à gradu medij cæli, & in revolutione annorum natiuitatum ad prenoſcendum efſſe nati à gradu afcendentis revolutionis, omnibus LIX.minutis Gocto fecundis per aſcenſiones regionis diem vnum. De dire&ionibus ſeu deductionibus fignificatorum. Et ex hoc Dirigere Latinis id quod Gręcis ευθύνομ,ἢ ἰθύνου το σκάλιους ſequitur hoceft:Obliquum emendare & rectum facere ſignificat: & diata.&c. setum iter eſt, quod in ambages non deflectit, fed dire&è,& vt Græ / Digitized by Google ==Start of OCR for page 4== " QUARTA 411 Graci loquuntur, επ' ευθείας progreditur. Hinc credo rece ptum effe, vt zodiaci obliquarum & inæqualium afcenfionum reductio ad regularé æqualemque menſură dicatur direrectio. Quonia enim tam in recta quàm in obliqua ſphæra, partes Ecli pticę inæqualiter peroriuntur & occidűt, fic vt alibi plus, alibi minus téporis aſcendendo deſcendédoq; abſumat, idcirco hæc anomalia ſeu inequalis inceſſus eclipticę, modò obliquè, modò rectè incedentis emendandus, atque ad certű regularemq; motum æquinoctialis, fingulis horis in omni ſphæræ situ præciſe part. 15. attollentis deprimentiſque conferendus eſt, vt euentuum tempora progreſsione partium cæli emenſa, certò queant cognofci.Nam quando ſingulę partes, fingulos annos in hac doctrina denunciare dicuntur, id non de Eclipticæ, vt Albumafar putabat, fed de partibus æquino&ialis intelligendum eſt. Verbi gratia, lignum Leonis antequàm totum Coloniæ afcendat, horas 2. fcrup.49 14 penè conſumit. At Aquarius vnica hora ſcrupuliſque 10 14 integer exoritur, ita vt partes Leonis, par tibus Aquarij longiorem tractum efficere nemo non intelligat.Etenim fignum Leonis aſcendendo trahit eo in loco equinoctialis partes 42.fcrup.23 14, Aquarij verò fignum faltem par tes 17.fcru.25 14. Quamobrem cùm in Ecliptica vnius quidem figni partes, longitudinem coorientium partium equinoctialis facilè vnicant, alterius verò ab eodem longius abſint(fingulienim gradus Aquarii annos non implent, Leonis verò gradus vnuſquiſque plus vno anno occupat) vtrunqueadvniformem & æqualem menſuram æquino&ialis meritò examinandúerit. Talem autem obliquæ & inæqualis progreſsionis emendationem, vulgò directionem nominant, quam vocem Pontanus tan quam ineptam ſæpius reiicit, atq; inambulationem profectio nemque dicendam eſſe precipit: Gręci wίπαμ& ἄφεσι dixerunt. Quidam wίπα generis vocem eſſe, atque cuiuflibet loci quamcunque progreſsionem fignificare volunt:ἄφεσιμ verò ſpeciem inambulationis, de qua hicagimus, qua prorogator & vitæ dominus Arabicè Ilech ditus,aliuſie ſignificator progrediés, ad obuios planetas aut eorúdé aſpectus motu equinotialis ſeu diurno deducitur, imò verò non ipſe prorogator, fed potius teſte Ptolemzo.partes interimentes, prorogatore fa immoDigitized by Google ==Start of OCR for page 5== 413 DIFFERENTIA immobiliterinEcliptica nonę ſphære fixo, & circulum poſitio mis ſuſtinente ad Aphetam deducuntur. Quotiefcunque erga prorogatore in ſua ſede, qua in geniturę cæli poſitu tenuit, comorantem, maleficarum corpora, aut radii quadrati vel oppofiti earundem inuadunt, διέρχεθ dicitur, id quod vulgò abſcin dere vocant.Nam malefici aut radii eorum minaces, quando hac via in vitę moderatorem aut horoſcopum incurrunt, vitam in cidunt, aut ſaltem diſcrimen corpori adferunt. Partes verò qua ſuo ad vitę dominum appulſu mortem aut periculum vitęadferre folent, μοίρας αναιρετικά's, id eft, partes lethales interimen teſque à loci malignitate vocari, fupra dictum eſt.Hęc ex ſenté. tia Ptolemei ita difcerni poſſunt:fed nos in expoſitione autoris docendi gratia feruabimus eius & reliquorum, qui poſtPtolemeum ſcripferunt, appellationes, quibus progreſsiones vniuerſe non vfque adeò malè, vt videtur, peculiaribus appellatio nibu
translation: And the place to which the direction arrives is called the place of division: the lord of the term of that place is called the divisor: if another planet or its rays or near the place before it is in the degree of division: this planet or the lord of the rays is said to be a participant of the divisor in the division.

translation: The significators which are directed are five places, that is, the degree of the ascendant, and the degree of the Sun, the degree of the Moon, the degree of Apheta, also the Part of Fortune, and the degree of the Midheaven. The climacteric degree of the ascendant is directed to discern accidents, which happen to the body: and the degree of the Sun to causes of dignity and exaltation: also of honor and kingdom: the Moon for the essence of the soul and body and marriage, the Part of Fortune for acquisitions and the smallness or generosity of profits, and the Midheaven for mastery and other particular dispositions and their essence.

Quonia enim tam in recta quàm in obliqua ſphæra, partes Ecli pticę inæqualiter peroriuntur & occidűt, fic vt alibi plus, alibi minus téporis aſcendendo deſcendédoq; abſumat, idcirco hæc anomalia ſeu inequalis inceſſus eclipticę, modò obliquè, modò rectè incedentis emendandus, atque ad certű regularemq; motum æquinoctialis, fingulis horis in omni ſphæræ situ præciſe part. 15. attollentis deprimentiſque conferendus eſt, vt euentuum tempora progreſsione partium cæli emenſa, certò queant cognofci.Nam quando ſingulę partes, fingulos annos in hac doctrina denunciare dicuntur, id non de Eclipticæ, vt Albumafar putabat, fed de partibus æquino&ialis intelligendum eſt. Verbi gratia, lignum Leonis antequàm totum Coloniæ afcendat, horas 2. fcrup.49 14 penè conſumit. At Aquarius vnica hora ſcrupuliſque 10 14 integer exoritur, ita vt partes Leonis, par tibus Aquarij longiorem tractum efficere nemo non intelligat.Etenim fignum Leonis aſcendendo trahit eo in loco equinoctialis partes 42.fcrup.23 14, Aquarij verò fignum faltem par tes 17.fcru.25 14. Quamobrem cùm in Ecliptica vnius quidem figni partes, longitudinem coorientium partium equinoctialis facilè vnicant, alterius verò ab eodem longius abſint(fingulienim gradus Aquarii annos non implent, Leonis verò gradus vnuſquiſque plus vno anno occupat) vtrunqueadvniformem & æqualem menſuram æquino&ialis meritò examinandúerit. Talem autem obliquæ & inæqualis progreſsionis emendationem, vulgò directionem nominant, quam vocem Pontanus tan quam ineptam ſæpius reiicit, atq; inambulationem profectio nemque dicendam eſſe precipit: Gręci wίπαμ& ἄφεσι dixerunt. Quotiefcunque erga prorogatore in ſua ſede, qua in geniturę cæli poſitu tenuit, comorantem, maleficarum corpora, aut radii quadrati vel oppofiti earundem inuadunt, διέρχεθ dicitur, id quod vulgò abſcin dere vocant.Nam malefici aut radii eorum minaces, quando hac via in vitę moderatorem aut horoſcopum incurrunt, vitam in cidunt, aut ſaltem diſcrimen corpori adferunt. Partes verò qua ſuo ad vitę dominum appulſu mortem aut periculum vitęadferre folent, μοίρας αναιρετικά's, id eft, partes lethales interimen teſque à loci malignitate vocari, fupra dictum eſt.Hęc ex ſenté. tia Ptolemei ita difcerni poſſunt:fed nos in expoſitione autoris docendi gratia feruabimus eius & reliquorum, qui poſtPtolemeum ſcripferunt, appellationes, quibus progreſsiones vniuerſe non vfque adeò malè, vt videtur, peculiaribus appellatio nibu
translation: But if it is the ascendant of the revolution of any years of the world or nativities, its significators are directed according to this direction, namely, one day for each degree. Some think that the significators of the year are directed in this way, namely, for every 59 minutes and 8 seconds, one day: for the direction of kings and princes is directed to them from the degree of the Midheaven through the ascensions of the direct circle, for every 59 minutes and 8 seconds, one day, until it reaches good or bad planets. And the essence of kings in health or illness is directed from the degree of the ascendant to good or bad planets for every 59 minutes and 8 seconds, one day through the ascensions of the region. And the direction in the revolution of the years of the world and to predict the essence of the common people or rustics from the degree of the ascendant: similarly and to predict the essence of the king from the degree of the Midheaven, and in the revolution of the years of nativities to predict the essence of the native from the degree of the ascendant of the revolution, for every 59 minutes and 8 seconds through the ascensions of the region, one day.

author: Valentin Naibod
title: On Directions or Deductions of Significators
text: ==Start of OCR for page 1== 408 BIFFERENTIA li direfli plus fignificatore regionis, mintes equationem de fignificatore circuli directi: & quod ex eo exierit,erit gradus directionis. 4 Quòd fi fuerit fignificator in aliqua quarta, locus ad quem diriges eum,in alia quarta, diriges fignificatorem ad angulum qui ei fuccedit:post hoc diriges ab angulo ad locum, quemadmodum oftendi tibi directionem ab angulo, & iunges vtraſque directiones. 5 Quòd cum ſciueris fignificatorem, volueris fcire quo perueniet directio de circulo ſignoru in aliquo annorum: fuerita significator in afcendente: addes numerum annorum fuper afcenfiones gradus afcendentis:& facies eum arcum in afcenfione regionis:et quo exiuerit arcus grad. de circulo fignorum, ibi pero ueniet directio in eodem anno. Si verò fuerit fignificator in VII. addes numerum annorum fuper afcenfiones in VII. addes numerum an nadir gradus fignificatoris : & arcuabis eum etiam in afcenfionibus regionis: & quod exierit de circulo fignorum, ad eius nadir perueniet directio eodem anno. Quòd fi fuerit fignificator in X.vel IIII.addes fuper afcenfior nes gradus fignificatoris in circulo directo numerum an norum: & arcuabis eum in afcefionibus circuli directi, &quod exierit de circulo ſignorum, ibidem perueniet in eodem anno directio. Si verò fuerit fignificator extra hac quatuor loca que funt anguli:fcias horas longitudi nis ab angulo, ficut prædixi: quòd fi fuerit fignificator in medietate orientali: addes fuper aſcenſiones grad.finificatoris per circulum directum numerum annorum quos volueris: & arcuabis koc in afcenfionibus circuli directi, Digitized by Google ==Start of OCR for page 2== QUARTA. 409: directi:& quod exierit, erit ſignificator circuli directi, ferua eum.Poft hoc addes numerum annorum etiam fue per afcenfiones grad.fignificatoris in regione: arcua bis hoc in aſcenſione regionis:& quod fuerit, erit ſignifi cator regionis. Poft hoc accipies fextam partem refidui inter fignificatorem circuli directi, ſignificatorem regionis: & multiplicabis eam in horas longitudinis ab angulo: quod fuerit, erit equatio. Quòd fifuerit figni ficator circuli directi minor ſignificatore regionis: ada des aquationem ſuper ſignificatorem circuli directi: fi fuerit plus, minues eam de eo:& quod remanferit, ibi perueniet directio ex circulo fignorum eodem anno. Quòd fi fuerit fignificator in medietate occidentali,fa. cies eum nadir grad. fignificatoris,ficut fecifti cum gra du fignificatoris in opere quod eſt ante eum:& quod fue vit, ibidem perueniet directio ex circulo fignorum in eo. dem anno. 6 Et locus ad quem perueniet directio, dici tur locus diuifionis: dominus termini illius loci,dici tur diuifor:quòd fi fuerit in gradu diuiſionis planeta a lius aut radij eius aut propè locum ante eum:dicitur hic planeta aut dominus radiorum,particeps diuiforis in dia uifione. 7 7 Significatores quoq; qui diriguntur, funt quinq; loca, id eft, gra.afcendentis, & gra. Solis gra. Luna, grad. Aphetæ quoque partis fortune, ac gradus medij cæli. Gradus climacto nanque afcendentis dirigitur ad daccidentia accidentia dignofcen. dignofcen rica. da, que accidunt in corpore:& grad. Solis ad dignitatis exaltationis caufas: honoris quoque atque regni : f : Luna Digitized by Google ==Start of OCR for page 3== 410 DIFFERENTIA Luna propter effe anime & corporis atque coniugy, pars fortune propter acquifitiones & profectus exigui tatem Glargitatem, & medium cæli propter magifteri. um & ceteras difpofitiones particulares & effe eorum. 8 Si autem fuerit afcendens revolutionis alicutus ane norum mundi vel natiuitatum, diriguntur ſignificato res eius fecundum hanc directionem,vnicuique fcilicet gradui diem vnum. Quibufdam videtur vt dirigantur fe gnificatores anni hoc modo, omnibus fcilicet LIX.mi. nutis VIII.fecundis vnum diem:nam directio regum principum fic dirigitur eis à gradumedy cæliper a ſceſiones circuli directi, omnibus LIX.minutis, er ofte fecundis vnum diem, donec perueniat ad bonos vel malos planetas. Et dirigitur effe regum in incolumitate, aut infirmitate ex gradu afcendentis ad banos vel malos planetas omnibus, LIX.minutis, & octo fecundis diem vnum per aſcenſiones regionis. Et directio in revolutio ne annorum mundi & prænoſcendum eſſe vulgi velru fticorum à gradu afcendentis : fimiliter & ad prenoſcen dum effe regis à gradu medij cæli, & in revolutione annorum natiuitatum ad prenoſcendum efſſe nati à gradu afcendentis revolutionis, omnibus LIX.minutis Gocto fecundis per aſcenſiones regionis diem vnum. De dire&ionibus ſeu deductionibus fignificatorum. translation: And from this, to direct in Latin is what the Greeks call ευθύνομ, ἢ ἰθύνου το σκάλιους, which means: to correct what is crooked and make it straight: and a straight path is that which does not deviate into windings, but proceeds directly, and as the Greeks say, επ' ευθείας. Hence I believe it has been accepted that the reduction of the oblique and unequal ascensions of the zodiac to a regular and equal measure is called direction. For since in both the right and oblique sphere, the parts of the Ecliptic rise and set unequally, so that sometimes more, sometimes less time is consumed in ascending and descending, therefore this anomaly or unequal progression of the Ecliptic, sometimes proceeding obliquely, sometimes directly, must be corrected, and compared to the certain and regular motion of the equinoctial, which precisely raises and lowers 15 parts in every hour in every position of the sphere, so that the times of events, measured by the progression of the parts of the sky, can be certainly known. For when individual parts are said to announce individual years in this doctrine, this is not to be understood of the Ecliptic, as Albumasar thought, but of the parts of the equinoctial. For example, the sign of Leo almost consumes 2 hours, 49 minutes, and 14 seconds before it entirely ascends in Cologne. But Aquarius rises completely in 1 hour and 10 minutes and 14 seconds, so that no one fails to understand that the parts of Leo produce a longer tract than the parts of Aquarius. For the sign of Leo, in ascending, draws 42 parts, 23 minutes, and 14 seconds of the equinoctial in that place, while the sign of Aquarius draws only 17 parts, 25 minutes, and 14 seconds. Therefore, since in the Ecliptic the parts of one sign easily unify the length of the co-rising parts of the equinoctial, while those of another are further from the same (for individual degrees of Aquarius do not complete years, but each degree of Leo occupies more than one year), both must be examined to a uniform and equal measure of the equinoctial. Such a correction of oblique and unequal progression is commonly called direction, a term which Pontanus often rejects as inappropriate, and prescribes that it should be called ambulation or progression: the Greeks called it wίπαμ& ἄφεσι. Some want wίπα to be a generic term, signifying any progression of any place: ἄφεσιμ, however, is a specific kind of ambulation, which we are discussing here, by which the prorogator and lord of life, called Ilech in Arabic, or any other progressing significator, is led to opposing planets or their aspects by equinoctial or diurnal motion, or rather, not the prorogator itself, but rather, according to Ptolemy, the intervening parts, with the prorogator fixed immovably in the Ecliptic of the ninth sphere, and sustaining the circle of position, are led to Apheta. Therefore, whenever the prorogator, remaining in its seat, which it held in the position of the nativity, is invaded by the bodies of malefics, or their square or opposite rays, it is called διέρχεθ, which is commonly called cutting off. For when malefics or their threatening rays encounter the moderator of life or the horoscope in this way, they cut off life, or at least bring danger to the body. But the parts which, by their approach to the lord of life, usually bring death or danger to life, are called μοίρας αναιρετικά's, that is, lethal and intervening parts, from the malignancy of the place, as was said above. These can be distinguished in this way according to Ptolemy's opinion: but we, in the exposition of the author, for the sake of teaching, will retain his and the other appellations of those who wrote after Ptolemy, by which universal progressions are not so badly, it seems, designated by peculiar appellations.

author: Valentin Naibod
title: Direction and Profection
text: s difcernuntur. Rationem 21ὰ ἀφέσεωμ, qua extemporibus circuli equino&ialis Catholici chronocratores ſumuntur, vocant directionem. Alterum verò modum progrediendi, quo annui & menſtrui diurnique temporum diſpoſitores ordinantur, profectionem nominant. Ac de profe&ionibus ſuperiore capite dictum eft: In hoc verò exponit autor leges directionú, quarum hec ſit deſcriptio. Dirigere eſt artificioſe inueftigare arcum equinoctialis inter ſignificatorem & promiflorem interceptum coaſcendentem vel condeſcentem arcui Ecliptica, refpectu circuli poſitionis,in quo alter eorum iacet, hoc eft: Dirigere nihil aliud eſt, quam videre, quot mundi gradus medium cælum fiue horizonta climatis, aliumúe circulum pofitio nis pretereant, dum ſtella ſeu locus primus ad ſtellam alteram vel ad alterius radios, aliumúe aliquem locum ſecundum ducitur. Nominamus autem hic, vt fuprà, circulů pofitionis per meridiani & horizontis interſectiones & fidus ductum. Significa torem feu Aphetam dicimus locum primum, ad quem ſecudus ducitur, atque vicem ſubiecti mutationem aliquam certo tempore à promiffore recepturi obtinet. Alterum verò qui ad prio rem progreditur,atque vicem cauſe agentis gerit, promifforem & Ancretam.Nam vt paulò antè monui, in directione dire&a ex fententia Ptolemei, Angreta ad locum apheticum, no autem Aphe Digitized by Google QUARTA. 415 Apheta ad Anæretam, vt vulgò putant, deducitur. Verba autem eius de partibus anereticis, aphetę in directione cõuerſa occurrentibus paulo poſt ſubiiciam, ex quibus patebit, quòd in direAione conuerfa, Apheta partes interimentes inuadit, vnde ſtatim conſequitur, in directione altera ſeu directa aneretas incur fu aphetam petere. Tempus verò, quod labitur dum locus qui- ſpiam cæli ad ſedem loci alterius promouetur, aut periodum vitę nati metitur, aut alterius cuiuſpiam rei euentum definit, ſi id in equinoctialis partes ſeu tépora reſoluatur, tot enim funt futuri anni, quot fuerint directionis equalia tempora. Quapropter quinq; ſcrupula menſem, fingula verò ſcrupula dies ſex & duas horas monftrabunt. Porre duæ funt ſpecies directionis: Vnam Regiomontanus problemate 25.directionem directam vocat, in qua locus ſecundus,id eft, promiſſor feuanæreta intel ligitur traduci ad ſitum primi, hoc eſt, ad ſede ſignificatoris ſeu apheta.Haciς τὰ ἑπόμlra, id eft, in conſequentiaſigna ſecun, dum afcenfiones ipſius aphetæ perficitur, & à Ptolemeo ἀκι- νοβολία,id eft, radiorum proiectio vocatur, quòd ſola radij deductione ad fimilem fitum llegij ſimpliciter tempus futuri eué- tus colligat. Alteram εἰς τὰ προαγόμλra, id eſt, in ſigna præceden tia, vocat conuerſam directionem, in qua locus Apheticus ad lo cum anæretæ ſecundum aſcenſiones ipſius aneretæ in ſignis an tecedentibus transfertur. Hanc directioné adhibent vates qui dam ad horoſcopualiaſque ſortes, & retrocedetes planetas deducentes. Sed Ptolemæum video hunc modum proprium face re vitæ fignificatori conſtituto inter cuspidem decimæ & fe ptima domus. Vult autem Ptolemæus cùm à tali situ vitæ fignificator εἰς τὰ προκγόμενα deducendus eſt, eŭ tantů locum, qui eſt in finitore occidentali, perimere, atque ob eam cauſam dato rem vitæ in locis occidentalibus, id eſt, à celi culmine & inter cardinem occiduum repertum, tam in conſequentia quàm præ cedentia figna agit. Verùm in hoc caſu quando Apheta motu diurno ad confimilem fitum ſtellæ aut radij in ſignis antecedentibus exiſtentis deducitur, tempus quod interea labitur, nő metitur moram futuri euentus, ſed tempus quo apheta ad centrum occafus fertur, auget aut diminuit, ratione temporum ho Fariorum loci planete, qui, vel cuius radij delabentem aphetam f 3 retar- 414 DIFFERENTIA retardant aut ocyus protrudunt, & ratione diſtantiz, qua idemm planeta ab occidente cardine diſtat. Nam radij aut planetarum corpora, qui ſunt ante Aphetam &inter cardinem occidentem, non occidunt, ſed ſi obuiantes fuerint benefici, tuncaphetæ ad occidentem progredientis lapſum inhibent, eique annos, mea ſes & dies adijciunt. Econtrariò ſi malefici fuerint, Aphetam ad cardinem occiduum tendentem ocyus & ante tempus detrudunt,eiq; annos, menſes &dies detrahunt:Mercurius verò vtris adiunctus fuerit, hos adiuuabit. Hæc directio ὡριμαία dicitur, quòd præter progreſsionis tempus, infuper obferuet dona & ſpolia locorum Aphetę decidenti obuiantium. Sed quantum. addant diminuantque, colligitur ex partium vniufcuiuſque fi tu. Quot enim fuerint horaria tempora vniufcuiuſque partis, id eſt, planetæ adijcientis vel diminuentis, interdiu diurna, noAu noturna, tot ſignificantur anni: qui ſanè numerus,cùm in ortu planeta beneficus repertus fuerit, integer addendus eft ad annos per progreſsionem Aphetæ ad occidentem collectos: vel ſubtrahendus eſt ab ijſdem, ſi maleficus & in ortu repertus fuerit. Quòd fi verò talis planeta non ſit in oriente, quot horis ab horoſcopo diſtiterit planeta addens vel minuens, tot dece dunt vel accedunt horarijs temporibus aſſes, ita vt in occafu in nihilum abeat, quod tempori prędicto addatur, vel ab eodem minuendum fit:quam quidem rem alioqui obſcuram,exempla ri diſcurſu planiorem reddemus. In noſtro exéplo Solem, cùm in loco octauo & in figno masculino, & denique in hexagono patico Saturni in parte Solis ius diurni trianguli exercétis deprehendatur, de ſententia Arabum non immeritò Aphetă pronunciare poteramus, nifi ipſiin hoc dominio longè præferendam putaremus partem horoſcopantem, idque autoritate Ptolemæi, qui planetis in octauo tanquàm otiofo loco commoran tibus,dominium vitæ difertè adimit. Cæterú nos docendi gratia fingamus eum eſſe vitæ moderatorem in medietate occiden tali politum, quandoquidem in exemplis non tam rei veritatem, quam regularum claram expoſitionem ſpectamus. Primò ergo iuxta regulam Ptolemei infrà explicandam,inquirenda ſunt tépora æquinoctialis, quibus Solad occaſum deuoluitur. Diſtantia Solis à cæli culmine eſt par. 56.fcrup.17 14, & horaria tem- 1
translation: They are distinguished. They call the method of 21 ἀφέσεωμ, by which the chronocrators are taken from the times of the equinoctial Catholic circle, a direction. The other method of progression, by which the annual, monthly, and daily disposers of times are ordered, they call profection. And profections were discussed in the previous chapter: In this chapter, however, the author explains the laws of directions, of which this is the description. To direct is to artificially investigate the arc of the equinoctial intercepted between the significator and the promittor, co-ascending or co-descending with the arc of the Ecliptic, with respect to the circle of position, in which one of them lies, that is: To direct is nothing other than to see how many degrees of the world the midheaven or the horizon of the climate, and another circle of position, pass, while a star or a first place is led to another star or to the rays of another, or to some other second place. We call here, as above, the circle of position by the intersections of the meridian and the horizon and the path of the star. We call the significator or Apheta the first place, to which the second is led, and which obtains the role of the subject, about to receive some change at a certain time from the promittor. The other, however, who progresses to the former, and who plays the role of the active cause, is called the promittor and Anareta. For, as I warned a little earlier, in direct direction according to Ptolemy's opinion, the Anareta is led to the aphetic place, not the Apheta to the Anareta, as is commonly thought. I will shortly subjoin his words concerning the anaretic parts, which occur in the converse direction of the apheta, from which it will be clear that in converse direction, the Apheta invades the destroying parts, whence it immediately follows that in the other or direct direction, the anaretas seek the apheta by their influence. The time, however, which elapses while some place of the sky moves to the seat of another place, either measures the period of the native's life, or defines the outcome of some other event, if it is resolved into parts or times of the equinoctial, for there will be as many future years as there are equal times of direction. Therefore, five scruples will show a month, and single scruples will show six days and two hours. Furthermore, there are two kinds of direction: One Regiomontanus in problem 25 calls direct direction, in which the second place, that is, the promittor or anareta, is understood to be led to the position of the first, that is, to the seat of the significator or apheta. This is done in the subsequent signs, according to the ascensions of the apheta itself, and by Ptolemy it is called ἀκινοβολία, that is, the projection of rays, because by the mere deduction of a ray to a similar position, it simply collects the time of a future event. The other, εἰς τὰ προαγόμλra, that is, into the preceding signs, he calls converse direction, in which the Aphetic place is transferred to the place of the anareta according to the ascensions of the anareta itself in the preceding signs. Some diviners use this direction for horoscopes and other lots, and for leading retrograde planets. But I see Ptolemy making this method proper to the significator of life constituted between the cusp of the tenth and seventh houses. Ptolemy wants, however, when the significator of life from such a position is to be led εἰς τὰ προκγόμενα, that only that place which is in the western terminator should be destroyed, and for that reason, the giver of life in western places, that is, found from the culmination of the sky and between the western cardinal point, acts both in subsequent and preceding signs. But in this case, when the Apheta is led by diurnal motion to a similar position of a star or ray existing in the preceding signs, the time that elapses in the meantime does not measure the delay of a future event, but the time by which the apheta is carried to the center of the setting, increases or decreases, by reason of the horary times of the place of the planet, which, or whose rays, retard or more quickly propel the descending apheta, and by reason of the distance by which the same planet is distant from the western cardinal point. For the rays or bodies of planets, which are before the Apheta and between the western cardinal point, do not set, but if they are benefic and opposing, then they inhibit the fall of the apheta progressing to the west, and add years, months, and days to it. Conversely, if they are malefic, they more quickly and prematurely push the apheta tending to the western cardinal point, and subtract years, months, and days from it: Mercury, however, to whichever it is joined, will help them. This direction is called ὡριμαία, because besides the time of progression, it also observes the gifts and spoils of the places opposing the falling Apheta. But how much they add or subtract is gathered from the parts of each position. For as many horary times as there are for each part, that is, of the planet adding or subtracting, diurnal by day, nocturnal by night, so many years are signified: which number, indeed, when a benefic planet is found in the ascendant, is to be added entirely to the years collected by the progression of the Apheta to the west: or is to be subtracted from them, if it is malefic and found in the ascendant. But if such a planet is not in the east, as many hours as the adding or subtracting planet is distant from the ascendant, so many parts of the horary times recede or accede, so that at the setting it comes to nothing, which is to be added to the predicted time, or to be subtracted from it: which matter, otherwise obscure, we will make clearer by an exemplary discussion. In our example, since the Sun is found in the eighth place and in a masculine sign, and finally in a paternal hexagon of Saturn exercising the right of the diurnal triplicity in the part of the Sun, according to the opinion of the Arabs, we could not undeservedly pronounce it the Apheta, unless we thought the horoscoping part to be far preferable in this dominion, and that by the authority of Ptolemy, who expressly denies the dominion of life to planets residing in the eighth house as an idle place. However, for the sake of teaching, let us imagine it to be the moderator of life placed in the western hemisphere, since in examples we look not so much at the truth of the matter as at a clear exposition of the rules. First, therefore, according to Ptolemy's rule to be explained below, the equinoctial times by which the Sun revolves to the setting must be inquired. The distance of the Sun from the culmination of the sky is 56 parts, 17 scruples, 14, and the horary times...

author: Valentin Naibod
title: Critique of Regiomontanus's Method
text: ==Start of OCR for page 1== 418 DIFFERENTIA hoc, quòd non appofuit rationem operationum, quam videmus in tabulis Alphonfi, adeo vt niſi ſit peritiſsimus Mathematicus, oportet vt perpetuò inhæreat regulæ & tabulæ monteregii, quod ipſe ſtudioſe fecit, vt homines eſſent ſerui libri eius, vnde iuſte à patre meo reprehendebatur. Nam homo fit obnoxius er rori trifariam.Primum, ſi tabula aliqua in parte mendoſa ſit.Secúdo, ſi ſiniſtrè aliquo modo regulam intelligas, quod facilè euenit inter tot varietates latitudinis borealis, auſtralis, afcenſio nis retz, obliquæ, arcus noturni vel diurni, totius vel medietatis in medietate deſcendente velaſcendente, ſupra vel infra terram:ob id meo iudicio optima eſt ſphæra ſolida magna, vel planiſphærium magnú, ad amuſsim ex are factum, &c. Sic ſanè doctissimi Regiomontani à pleriſque laudatiſsimam dirigendi rationem Cardanus propter operandi difficultatem parum pro bare, atque in poſtrema ætate de artis veritate cófirmatus, rarò vel nunquam vfurpaſſe videtur. Sed enim prolixius hæc diſputare non eſt noſtri inſtituti, quibus non tam quid in hoc nego tio verum fit exponere, quam planam dirigendi formam facere eſt propoſitum. Primò ergo, ve ab autoris modo dirigendi incipiamus, admonemur hocloco, ante omnia diligenter cogno- ſcendum eſſe, in qua parte cœliplaneta ſignificator conſtitutus fit, cuius varius atque in diuerfis locis pofitus efficit, vt in colligendis temporibus nunc ſpheræ aſconſiones, nunc verò climatis,in quo conſtituti fuerimus, nobis attendendæ fint. Nam fi fignificator pofitus fuerit in cardine exorientis, iuxta climatis aſcenſiones, fi cardinem occiduum tenuerit, iuxta climatis de- ſcenſiones, ſi denique cardinem medium velimum cæli occupauerit,iuxta ſphæræ recta aſcenſiones tempora futurorum euentuum metienda atque ſupputanda ſunt. Quapropter ſi ſignificatorem in afcendente locatum,ad locum aliquem ſubſequentis promiſſoris dirigere volueris, aufer ſignificatoris afcen fiones obliquas ab aſcenſionibus obliquis promiſſoris, hoc efty. principium arcus ſubtrahe à fine:id enim quod remanet,arcum dire&ionis oftendet. Verbi gratia, fi o'ex Capricorno feriat quadrangulariter part. 12. Deinde ſimili modo capias aſcenſionem obliquam ſignificatoris, nempe par.5. X, quç eſt par.349.fcrup. 213. Tertio fubtrahe poſteriorem afcenfionem, nempe ſignificatoris, quæ eſt par.349. fcrup. 2 r", ab afcenfione priori,hoceft, promiſſoris, que eſt par.5.fcrup.81, accommodata integra reuolutione feu par.360.id enim ſemper faciendű eſt, quoties in calculo phyſico inferior ſuperiorem excellit,atque ab eo ob id auferri non poteſt: quo facto remanebunt directionis quæfitæ partes 16.fcrup.6 14. Quòd ſi ſignificator fue xit in angulo occidentis, fubtrahe deſcenſionem obliquam ſi. gnificatoris à deſcenſione obliqua promiſſoris,&relinquentur. tempora directionis. Nec aliud eft, quod autor præcipit, minu endam eſſe aſcenſionem obliquam puncti loco fignificatoris diametraliter oppoſiti(id enim Arabes nadir vocant) ab aſcenfione obliqua punti, quod loco promiſſoris ex diametro obijcitur.Nam in ſphæra obliqua aſcenſio ſigni, eſt deſcenſioſigni. oppofiti,&econtrario deſcenſio ſigni, eſt aſcenſio ſigni oppoſi ti, vt in priori exemplo: Si 14. in cardine occiduo exiſtat, atq; ad radium dirigendus sit: primò quærenda eſt promifforis, hoceft,par.12.V deſcenſio, ſeu quod idem eſt, partium 12.figni oppoſiti, id eſt, Libre inuenienda eſt obliqua aſcenſio, quzeſt par. 196.fcrup.5514. Deinde fimiliter excerpenda eſt ſignifica toris,hoceft, par.5.X deſcenſio,hoceft, oppoſiti ſigni np. par.5. aſcenſio, quæ eſt par. 144. fcrup.40 19, quæ fubtracta à priori, re Linquit quæfita tempora directionis par.52.fcrup.15 14.Adextremum ſi ſignificator in medio aut imo coeli fuerit, afcenfio recta ſignificatoris auferenda eſt ab aſcenſione recta promiſſosis, vt quæfita tempora dire&ionis exeant. Exemplum: In eadé regionis altitudine par.5 1. fuit cuiufdam genituræ culminans par. 16.95, ignificator ſcilicet honoris & magiſteriſ, quem ad A Solis, id eft,ad promiſſorem volo dirigere, qui erat in par.9.2 ſubtrahe ergo afcenſionem rectam fignificatoris, quæ eſt par. 107.fcrup.22 14,abaſcenſione recta promiſſoris,quæ eftp.131. fcrupu.27 14,& remanent part.24.fcrupu. 14, directio M.C.ad trigonum Solis quæfita.
